units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
I shall not dine at home,
I meet the captains at the citadel.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Why, then, tomorrow night, or Tuesday morn.
On Tuesday noon, or night, or Wednesday morn.
I prithee name the time, but let it not
Exceed three days. In faith, he’s penitent,
And yet his trespass, in our common reason
(Save that, they say, the wars must make example
Out of her best) is not, almost, a fault
T' incur a private check. When shall he come?
Tell me, Othello. I wonder in my soul
What you would ask me that I should deny
Or stand so mamm'ring on. What? Michael Cassio
That came a-wooing with you, and so many a time,
When I have spoke of you dispraisingly,
Hath ta'en your part, to have so much to do
To bring him in? Trust me, I could do much—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Prithee, no more. Let him come when he will,
I will deny thee nothing.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Why, this is not a boon,
'Tis as I should entreat you wear your gloves,
Or feed on nourishing dishes, or keep you warm,
Or sue to you to do a peculiar profit
To your own person. Nay, when I have a suit
Wherein I mean to touch your love indeed
It shall be full of poise and difficult weight
And fearful to be granted.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
I will deny thee nothing!
Whereon I do beseech thee, grant me this,
To leave me but a little to myself.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Shall I deny you? No. Farewell, my lord.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Farewell, my Desdemona. I’ll come to thee straight.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Emilia, come.—Be as your fancies teach you.
Whate'er you be, I am obedient.
Exeunt DESDEMONA and EMILIA

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Excellent wretch! Perdition catch my soul
But I do love thee! And when I love thee not
Chaos is come again.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: My noble lord—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What dost thou say, Iago?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Did Michael Cassio, when you wooed my lady,
Know of your love?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
He did, from first to last.
Why dost thou ask?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
But for a satisfaction of my thought,
No further harm.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Why of thy thought, Iago?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: I did not think he had been acquainted with her.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Oh, yes, and went between us very oft.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Indeed?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Indeed? Ay, indeed! Discern’st thou aught in that?
Is he not honest?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Honest, my lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Honest, ay, honest.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: My lord, for aught I know.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What dost thou think?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Think, my lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
“Think, my lord?” Alas, thou echo’st me
As if there were some monster in thy thought
Too hideous to be shown. Thou dost mean something.
I heard thee say even now thou lik’st not that
When Cassio left my wife. What didst not like?
And when I told thee he was of my counsel
Of my whole course of wooing, thou cried’st “Indeed?”
And didst contract and purse thy brow together
As if thou then hadst shut up in thy brain
Some horrible conceit. If thou dost love me
Show me thy thought.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: My lord, you know I love you.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
I think thou dost.
And for I know thou 'rt full of love and honesty
And weigh’st thy words before thou giv’st them breath,
Therefore these stops of thine fright me the more.
For such things in a false disloyal knave
Are tricks of custom, but in a man that’s just
They are close dilations, working from the heart,
That passion cannot rule.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
For Michael Cassio,
I dare be sworn, I think, that he is honest.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I think so too.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Men should be what they seem,
Or those that be not, would they might seem none!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Certain, men should be what they seem.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Why then I think Cassio’s an honest man.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Nay, yet there’s more in this.
I prithee speak to me as to thy thinkings,
As thou dost ruminate, and give thy worst of thoughts
The worst of words.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Good my lord, pardon me,
Though I am bound to every act of duty
I am not bound to that all slaves are free to.
Utter my thoughts? Why, say they are vile and false,
As where’s that palace whereinto foul things
Sometimes intrude not? Who has that breast so pure
Wherein uncleanly apprehensions
Keep leets and law-days and in sessions sit
With meditations lawful?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Thou dost conspire against thy friend, Iago,
If thou but think’st him wronged and mak’st his ear
A stranger to thy thoughts.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I do beseech you,
Though I perchance am vicious in my guess,
As, I confess, it is my nature’s plague
To spy into abuses, and oft my jealousy
Shapes faults that are not, that your wisdom,
From one that so imperfectly conceits,
Would take no notice, nor build yourself a trouble
Out of his scattering and unsure observance.
It were not for your quiet nor your good,
Nor for my manhood, honesty, and wisdom
To let you know my thoughts.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What dost thou mean?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Good name in man and woman, dear my lord,
Is the immediate jewel of their souls.
Who steals my purse steals trash. 'Tis something,
nothing:
'Twas mine, ’tis his, and has been slave to thousands.
But he that filches from me my good name
Robs me of that which not enriches him
And makes me poor indeed.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I’ll know thy thoughts.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
You cannot, if my heart were in your hand,
Nor shall not, whilst ’tis in my custody.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Ha!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Oh, beware, my lord, of jealousy!
It is the green-eyed monster which doth mock
The meat it feeds on. That cuckold lives in bliss
Who, certain of his fate, loves not his wronger,
But, oh, what damnèd minutes tells he o'er
Who dotes, yet doubts— suspects, yet soundly loves!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Oh, misery!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Poor and content is rich, and rich enough,
But riches fineless is as poor as winter
To him that ever fears he shall be poor.
Good heaven, the souls of all my tribe defend
From jealousy!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Why, why is this?
Think’st thou I’d make a life of jealousy,
To follow still the changes of the moon
With fresh suspicions? No! To be once in doubt
Is to be resolved. Exchange me for a goat
When I shall turn the business of my soul
To such exsufflicate and blowed surmises,
Matching thy inference. 'Tis not to make me jealous
To say my wife is fair, feeds well, loves company,
Is free of speech, sings, plays, and dances.
Where virtue is, these are more virtuous.
Nor from mine own weak merits will I draw
The smallest fear or doubt of her revolt,
For she had eyes and chose me. No, Iago,
I’ll see before I doubt, when I doubt, prove,
And on the proof there is no more but this:
Away at once with love or jealousy!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I am glad of this, for now I shall have reason
To show the love and duty that I bear you
With franker spirit. Therefore, as I am bound,
Receive it from me. I speak not yet of proof.
Look to your wife, observe her well with Cassio.
Wear your eyes thus, not jealous nor secure.
I would not have your free and noble nature
Out of self-bounty be abused. Look to ’t.
I know our country disposition well.
In Venice they do let God see the pranks
They dare not show their husbands. Their best
conscience
Is not to leave ’t undone, but keep’t unknown.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Dost thou say so?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
She did deceive her father, marrying you,
And when she seemed to shake and fear your looks,
She loved them most.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: And so she did.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Why, go to then.
She that, so young, could give out such a seeming,
To seel her father’s eyes up close as oak,
He thought ’twas witchcraft. But I am much to blame.
I humbly do beseech you of your pardon
For too much loving you.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I am bound to thee forever.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: I see this hath a little dashed your spirits.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Not a jot, not a jot.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Trust me, I fear it has.
I hope you will consider what is spoke
Comes from my love. But I do see you’re moved.
I am to pray you not to strain my speech
To grosser issues nor to larger reach
Than to suspicion.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I will not.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Should you do so, my lord,
My speech should fall into such vile success
Which my thoughts aimed not at. Cassio’s my worthy
friend—
My lord, I see you’re moved.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
No, not much moved.
I do not think but Desdemona’s honest.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Long live she so. And long live you to think so.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: And yet how nature, erring from itself—

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Ay, there’s the point. As, to be bold with you,
Not to affect many proposèd matches
Of her own clime, complexion, and degree,
Whereto we see in all things nature tends—
Foh! One may smell in such a will most rank,
Foul disproportions, thoughts unnatural.
But—pardon me— I do not in position
Distinctly speak of her, though I may fear
Her will, recoiling to her better judgment,
May fall to match you with her country forms,
And happily repent.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Farewell, farewell.
If more thou dost perceive, let me know more.
Set on thy wife to observe. Leave me, Iago.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: My lord, I take my leave. [going]

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
[aside] Why did I marry? This honest creature doubtless
Sees and knows more, much more, than he unfolds.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
(returns) My lord, I would I might entreat your honor
To scan this thing no farther. Leave it to time.
Although ’tis fit that Cassio have his place,
For sure, he fills it up with great ability,
Yet, if you please to hold him off awhile,
You shall by that perceive him and his means.
Note if your lady strain his entertainment
With any strong or vehement importunity.
Much will be seen in that. In the meantime,
Let me be thought too busy in my fears—
As worthy cause I have to fear I am—
And hold her free, I do beseech your honor.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Fear not my government.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: I once more take my leave.
Exit

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
This fellow’s of exceeding honesty
And knows all quantities, with a learnèd spirit,
Of human dealings. If I do prove her haggard,
Though that her jesses were my dear heartstrings,
I’d whistle her off and let her down the wind
To prey at fortune. Haply, for I am black
And have not those soft parts of conversation
That chamberers have, or for I am declined
Into the vale of years—yet that’s not much—
She’s gone, I am abused, and my relief
Must be to loathe her. Oh, curse of marriage
That we can call these delicate creatures ours
And not their appetites! I had rather be a toad
And live upon the vapor of a dungeon
Than keep a corner in the thing I love
For others' uses. Yet ’tis the plague to great ones,
Prerogatived are they less than the base.
'Tis destiny unshunnable, like death.
Even then this forkèd plague is fated to us
When we do quicken. Look where she comes.
Enter DESDEMONA and EMILIA

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
If she be false, heaven mocked itself.
I’ll not believe ’t.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
How now, my dear Othello?
Your dinner, and the generous islanders
By you invited, do attend your presence.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I am to blame.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Why do you speak so faintly?
Are you not well?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I have a pain upon my forehead, here.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Why that’s with watching, ’twill away again.
Let me but bind it hard, within this hour
It will be well. [pulls out a handkerchief]

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Your napkin is too little,
Let it alone.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I am very sorry that you are not well.
Exeunt OTHELLO and DESDEMONA

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
(picks up the handkerchief)
I am glad I have found this napkin,
This was her first remembrance from the Moor.
My wayward husband hath a hundred times
Wooed me to steal it, but she so loves the token
(For he conjured her she should ever keep it)
That she reserves it evermore about her
To kiss and talk to. I’ll have the work ta'en out
And give ’t Iago. What he will do with it
Heaven knows, not I.
I nothing but to please his fantasy.
Enter IAGO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: How now! What do you here alone?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Do not you chide. I have a thing for you.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: A thing for me? It is a common thing—

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Ha?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: To have a foolish wife.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
Oh, is that all? What will you give me now
For the same handkerchief?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: What handkerchief?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
What handkerchief?
Why, that the Moor first gave to Desdemona,
That which so often you did bid me steal.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Hast stolen it from her?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
No, but she let it drop by negligence
And, to th' advantage, I being here, took ’t up.
Look, here it is.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: A good wench, give it me.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
What will you do with ’t, that you have been so earnest
To have me filch it?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Why, what is that to you?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
If it be not for some purpose of import,
Give ’t me again. Poor lady, she’ll run mad
When she shall lack it.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Be not acknown on ’t,
I have use for it. Go, leave me.
Exit EMILIA

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I will in Cassio’s lodging lose this napkin
And let him find it. Trifles light as air
Are to the jealous confirmations strong
As proofs of holy writ. This may do something.
The Moor already changes with my poison.
Dangerous conceits are in their natures poisons
Which at the first are scarce found to distaste,
But with a little act upon the blood
Burn like the mines of sulfur.
Enter OTHELLO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I did say so.
Look, where he comes. Not poppy nor mandragora
Nor all the drowsy syrups of the world,
Shall ever medicine thee to that sweet sleep
Which thou owedst yesterday.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Ha! Ha! False to me?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Why, how now, general? No more of that.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Avaunt! Be gone! Thou hast set me on the rack.
I swear ’tis better to be much abused
Than but to know ’t a little.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: How now, my lord!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
What sense had I in her stol'n hours of lust?
I saw ’t not, thought it not, it harmed not me.
I slept the next night well, fed well, was free and
merry.
I found not Cassio’s kisses on her lips.
He that is robbed, not wanting what is stol'n,
Let him not know’t, and he’s not robbed at all.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: I am sorry to hear this.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
I had been happy if the general camp,
Pioneers and all, had tasted her sweet body,
So I had nothing known. Oh, now forever
Farewell the tranquil mind! Farewell content!
Farewell the plumèd troops and the big wars
That makes ambition virtue! Oh, farewell!
Farewell the neighing steed and the shrill trump,
The spirit-stirring drum, th' ear-piercing fife,
The royal banner, and all quality,
Pride, pomp, and circumstance of glorious war!
And O you mortal engines, whose rude throats
The immortal Jove’s dead clamors counterfeit,
Farewell! Othello’s occupation’s gone.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Is ’t possible, my lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Villain, be sure thou prove my love a whore,
Be sure of it. Give me the ocular proof
Or by the worth of mine eternal soul
Thou hadst been better have been born a dog
Than answer my waked wrath!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Is ’t come to this?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Make me to see ’t, or at the least so prove it
That the probation bear no hinge nor loop
To hang a doubt on, or woe upon thy life!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: My noble lord—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
If thou dost slander her and torture me,
Never pray more. Abandon all remorse.
On horror’s head horrors accumulate,
Do deeds to make heaven weep, all earth amazed,
For nothing canst thou to damnation add
Greater than that.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Oh, grace! Oh, heaven forgive me!
Are you a man? Have you a soul or sense?
God buy you, take mine office. O wretched fool
That lov’st to make thine honesty a vice!
O monstrous world! Take note, take note, O world,
To be direct and honest is not safe.
I thank you for this profit, and from hence
I’ll love no friend, sith love breeds such offence.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Nay, stay. Thou shouldst be honest.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I should be wise, for honesty’s a fool
And loses that it works for.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
By the world,
I think my wife be honest and think she is not.
I think that thou art just and think thou art not.
I’ll have some proof. Her name, that was as fresh
As Dian’s visage, is now begrimed and black
As mine own face. If there be cords or knives,
Poison, or fire, or suffocating streams,
I’ll not endure it. Would I were satisfied!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I see, sir, you are eaten up with passion.
I do repent me that I put it to you.
You would be satisfied?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Would? Nay, and I will.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
And may, but how? How satisfied, my lord?
Would you, the supervisor, grossly gape on,
Behold her topped?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Death and damnation! Oh!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
It were a tedious difficulty, I think,
To bring them to that prospect. Damn them then,
If ever mortal eyes do see them bolster
More than their own! What then? How then?
What shall I say? Where’s satisfaction?
It is impossible you should see this,
Were they as prime as goats, as hot as monkeys,
As salt as wolves in pride, and fools as gross
As ignorance made drunk. But yet, I say,
If imputation and strong circumstances
Which lead directly to the door of truth
Will give you satisfaction, you may have ’t.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Give me a living reason she’s disloyal.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I do not like the office.
But, sith I am entered in this cause so far,
Pricked to ’t by foolish honesty and love,
I will go on. I lay with Cassio lately
And, being troubled with a raging tooth,
I could not sleep. There are a kind of men
So loose of soul that in their sleeps will mutter
Their affairs. One of this kind is Cassio.
In sleep I heard him say “Sweet Desdemona,
Let us be wary, let us hide our loves.”
And then, sir, would he gripe and wring my hand,
Cry “O sweet creature!” and then kiss me hard,
As if he plucked up kisses by the roots
That grew upon my lips, lay his leg
Over my thigh, and sigh, and kiss, and then
Cry “Cursed fate that gave thee to the Moor!”

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Oh, monstrous! Monstrous!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Nay, this was but his dream.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: But this denoted a foregone conclusion.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
'Tis a shrewd doubt, though it be but a dream.
And this may help to thicken other proofs
That do demonstrate thinly.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I’ll tear her all to pieces!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Nay, yet be wise, yet we see nothing done,
She may be honest yet. Tell me but this,
Have you not sometimes seen a handkerchief
Spotted with strawberries in your wife’s hand?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I gave her such a one, ’twas my first gift.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I know not that, but such a handkerchief—
I am sure it was your wife’s—did I today
See Cassio wipe his beard with.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: If it be that—

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
If it be that, or any that was hers,
It speaks against her with the other proofs.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Oh, that the slave had forty thousand lives!
One is too poor, too weak for my revenge.
Now do I see ’tis true. Look here, Iago,
All my fond love thus do I blow to heaven—'tis gone.
Arise, black vengeance, from the hollow hell!
Yield up, O love, thy crown and hearted throne
To tyrannous hate! Swell, bosom, with thy fraught,
For ’tis of aspics' tongues!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Yet be content.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Oh, blood, blood, blood!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Patience, I say. Your mind may change.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Never, Iago. Like to the Pontic sea,
Whose icy current and compulsive course
Ne'er keeps retiring ebb but keeps due on
To the Propontic and the Hellespont,
Even so my bloody thoughts with violent pace
Shall ne'er look back, ne'er ebb to humble love
Till that a capable and wide revenge
Swallow them up. Now, by yon marble heaven,
In the due reverence of a sacred vow
I here engage my words. (he kneels)

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Do not rise yet.
Witness, you ever-burning lights above,
You elements that clip us round about,
Witness that here Iago doth give up
The execution of his wit, hands, heart,
To wronged Othello’s service. Let him command,
And to obey shall be in me remorse,
What bloody business ever.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
I greet thy love
Not with vain thanks but with acceptance bounteous,
And will upon the instant put thee to ’t.
Within these three days let me hear thee say
That Cassio’s not alive.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
My friend is dead,
'Tis done at your request. But let her live.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Damn her, lewd minx! Oh, damn her, damn her!
Come, go with me apart. I will withdraw
To furnish me with some swift means of death
For the fair devil. Now art thou my lieutenant.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: I am your own for ever.
Exeunt
Enter DESDEMONA, EMILIA, and CLOWN

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Do you know, sirrah, where Lieutenant Cassio lies?

units.speaker: CLOWN
units.text: I dare not say he lies anywhere.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Why, man?

units.speaker: CLOWN
units.text:
He’s a soldier, and for one to say a soldier lies,
’tis stabbing.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Go to. Where lodges he?

units.speaker: CLOWN
units.text: To tell you where he lodges is to tell you where I lie.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Can anything be made of this?

units.speaker: CLOWN
units.text:
I know not where he lodges, and for me to devise a
lodging and say he lies here, or he lies there, were to
lie in mine own throat.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Can you inquire him out and be edified by report?

units.speaker: CLOWN
units.text:
I will catechize the world for him, that is, make
questions, and by them answer.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Seek him, bid him come hither. Tell him I have moved my
lord on his behalf, and hope all will be well.

units.speaker: CLOWN
units.text:
To do this is within the compass of man’s wit, and
therefore I will attempt the doing it.
Exit

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Where should I lose that handkerchief, Emilia?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: I know not, madam.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Believe me, I had rather have lost my purse
Full of crusadoes. And but my noble Moor
Is true of mind and made of no such baseness
As jealous creatures are, it were enough
To put him to ill thinking.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Is he not jealous?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Who, he? I think the sun where he was born
Drew all such humors from him.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Look where he comes.
Enter OTHELLO

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
I will not leave him now till Cassio
Be called to him.—How is ’t with you, my lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Well, my good lady.— [aside] Oh, hardness to dissemble!—
How do you, Desdemona?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Well, my good lord.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Give me your hand. This hand is moist, my lady.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: It hath felt no age nor known no sorrow.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
This argues fruitfulness and liberal heart.
Hot, hot, and moist. This hand of yours requires
A sequester from liberty, fasting, and prayer,
Much castigation, exercise devout,
For here’s a young and sweating devil here,
That commonly rebels. 'Tis a good hand,
A frank one.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
You may indeed say so,
For ’twas that hand that gave away my heart.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
A liberal hand. The hearts of old gave hands,
But our new heraldry is hands, not hearts.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I cannot speak of this. Come now, your promise.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What promise, chuck?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I have sent to bid Cassio come speak with you.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
I have a salt and sorry rheum offends me.
Lend me thy handkerchief.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Here, my lord.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: That which I gave you.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I have it not about me.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Not?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: No, indeed, my lord.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
That’s a fault. That handkerchief
Did an Egyptian to my mother give,
She was a charmer and could almost read
The thoughts of people. She told her, while she kept it
'Twould make her amiable and subdue my father
Entirely to her love, but if she lost it
Or made gift of it, my father’s eye
Should hold her loathèd and his spirits should hunt
After new fancies. She, dying, gave it me
And bid me, when my fate would have me wived,
To give it her. I did so, and take heed on ’t,
Make it a darling like your precious eye.
To lose ’t or give ’t away were such perdition
As nothing else could match.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Is ’t possible?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
'Tis true. There’s magic in the web of it.
A sibyl, that had numbered in the world
The sun to course two hundred compasses,
In her prophetic fury sewed the work.
The worms were hallowed that did breed the silk,
And it was dyed in mummy which the skillful
Conserved of maidens' hearts.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Indeed? Is ’t true?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Most veritable, therefore look to ’t well.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Then would to Heaven that I had never seen ’t!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Ha! Wherefore?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Why do you speak so startingly and rash?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Is ’t lost? Is ’t gone? Speak, is ’t out o' th' way?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Bless us!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Say you?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: It is not lost, but what and if it were?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: How!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I say, it is not lost.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Fetch ’t, let me see ’t.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Why, so I can, sir, but I will not now.
This is a trick to put me from my suit.
Pray you, let Cassio be received again.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Fetch me the handkerchief—my mind misgives.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Come, come,
You’ll never meet a more sufficient man.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: The handkerchief!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
A man that all his time
Hath founded his good fortunes on your love,
Shared dangers with you—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: The handkerchief!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: In sooth, you are to blame.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Zounds!
Exit

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Is not this man jealous?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
I ne'er saw this before.
Sure, there’s some wonder in this handkerchief,
I am most unhappy in the loss of it.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
'Tis not a year or two shows us a man.
They are all but stomachs, and we all but food.
To eat us hungerly, and when they are full,
They belch us. Look you, Cassio and my husband!
Enter CASSIO and IAGO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
There is no other way. 'Tis she must do ’t,
And, lo, the happiness! Go and importune her.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: How now, good Cassio, what’s the news with you?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
Madam, my former suit. I do beseech you
That by your virtuous means I may again
Exist, and be a member of his love
Whom I, with all the office of my heart
Entirely honor. I would not be delayed.
If my offence be of such mortal kind
That nor my service past, nor present sorrows,
Nor purposed merit in futurity,
Can ransom me into his love again,
But to know so must be my benefit.
So shall I clothe me in a forced content,
And shut myself up in some other course,
To fortune’s alms.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Alas, thrice-gentle Cassio,
My advocation is not now in tune.
My lord is not my lord, nor should I know him
Were he in favor as in humor altered.
So help me every spirit sanctified
As I have spoken for you all my best
And stood within the blank of his displeasure
For my free speech. You must awhile be patient.
What I can do I will, and more I will
Than for myself I dare. Let that suffice you.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Is my lord angry?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
He went hence but now,
And certainly in strange unquietness.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Can he be angry? I have seen the cannon
When it hath blown his ranks into the air
And, like the devil, from his very arm
Puffed his own brother— and is he angry?
Something of moment then, I will go meet him.
There’s matter in ’t indeed, if he be angry.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I prithee, do so.
Exit IAGO

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Something, sure, of state,
Either from Venice, or some unhatched practice
Made demonstrable here in Cyprus to him,
Hath puddled his clear spirit, and in such cases
Men’s natures wrangle with inferior things,
Though great ones are their object. 'Tis even so,
For let our finger ache and it endues
Our other healthful members even to that sense
Of pain. Nay, we must think men are not gods,
Nor of them look for such observances
As fit the bridal. Beshrew me much, Emilia,
I was, unhandsome warrior as I am,
Arraigning his unkindness with my soul,
But now I find I had suborned the witness,
And he’s indicted falsely.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
Pray heaven it be
State matters, as you think, and no conception
Nor no jealous toy concerning you.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Alas the day! I never gave him cause.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
But jealous souls will not be answered so.
They are not ever jealous for the cause,
But jealous for they’re jealous. It is a monster
Begot upon itself, born on itself.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Heaven keep the monster from Othello’s mind!

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Lady, amen.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
I will go seek him.—Cassio, walk hereabout.
If I do find him fit, I’ll move your suit
And seek to effect it to my uttermost.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: I humbly thank your ladyship.
Exeunt DESDEMONA and EMILIA
Enter BIANCA

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Save you, friend Cassio!

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
What make you from home?
How is ’t with you, my most fair Bianca?
Indeed, sweet love, I was coming to your house.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text:
And I was going to your lodging, Cassio.
What, keep a week away? Seven days and nights?
Eight score eight hours? And lovers' absent hours
More tedious than the dial eightscore times!
Oh weary reckoning!

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
Pardon me, Bianca,
I have this while with leaden thoughts been pressed,
But I shall, in a more continuate time,
Strike off this score of absence. Sweet Bianca,
(giving her DESDEMONA’s handkerchief)
Take me this work out.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text:
O Cassio, whence came this?
This is some token from a newer friend!
To the felt absence now I feel a cause.
Is ’t come to this? Well, well.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
Go to, woman,
Throw your vile guesses in the devil’s teeth
From whence you have them. You are jealous now
That this is from some mistress, some remembrance.
No, in good troth, Bianca.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Why, whose is it?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
I know not neither, I found it in my chamber.
I like the work well. Ere it be demanded,
As like enough it will, I would have it copied.
Take it and do ’t, and leave me for this time.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Leave you! Wherefore?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
I do attend here on the general
And think it no addition, nor my wish,
To have him see me womaned.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Why, I pray you?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Not that I love you not.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text:
But that you do not love me.
I pray you bring me on the way a little
And say if I shall see you soon at night.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
'Tis but a little way that I can bring you,
For I attend here. But I’ll see you soon.

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: 'Tis very good. I must be circumstanced.
Exeunt
Enter OTHELLO and IAGO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Will you think so?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Think so, Iago?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
What,
To kiss in private?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: An unauthorized kiss!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Or to be naked with her friend in bed
An hour or more, not meaning any harm?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Naked in bed, Iago, and not mean harm!
It is hypocrisy against the devil.
They that mean virtuously, and yet do so,
The devil their virtue tempts, and they tempt heaven.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
So they do nothing, ’tis a venial slip.
But if I give my wife a handkerchief—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What then?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Why then ’tis hers, my lord, and, being hers,
She may, I think, bestow ’t on any man.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
She is protectress of her honor too.
May she give that?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Her honor is an essence that’s not seen,
They have it very oft that have it not.
But for the handkerchief—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
By heaven, I would most gladly have forgot it.
Thou saidst—Oh, it comes o'er my memory,
As doth the raven o'er the infectious house,
Boding to all—he had my handkerchief.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Ay, what of that?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: That’s not so good now.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
What if I had said I had seen him do you wrong?
Or heard him say—as knaves be such abroad,
Who having, by their own importunate suit,
Or voluntary dotage of some mistress,
Convincèd or supplied them, cannot choose
But they must blab—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Hath he said any thing?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
He hath, my lord, but be you well assured
No more than he’ll unswear.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What hath he said?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Why, that he did—I know not what he did.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What? what?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Lie—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: With her?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: With her, on her, what you will.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Lie with her? lie on her? We say “lie on her” when they
belie her! Lie with her—that’s fulsome.
Handkerchief—confessions—handkerchief! To confess, and
be hanged for his labor. First to be hanged, and then to
confess—I tremble at it . Nature would not invest
herself in such shadowing passion without some
instruction. It is not words that shake me thus. Pish!
Noses, ears, and lips. Is ’t possible?
Confess!—Handkerchief!—Oh, devil!—
Falls in a trance

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Work on, My medicine, work! Thus credulous fools are
caught,
And many worthy and chaste dames even thus,
All guiltless, meet reproach.— What, ho! My lord!
My lord, I say! Othello!
Enter CASSIO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: How now, Cassio!

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: What’s the matter?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
My lord is fall'n into an epilepsy.
This is his second fit. He had one yesterday.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Rub him about the temples.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
No, forbear.
The lethargy must have his quiet course.
If not, he foams at mouth and by and by
Breaks out to savage madness. Look, he stirs.
Do you withdraw yourself a little while,
He will recover straight. When he is gone
I would on great occasion speak with you.
Exit CASSIO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: How is it, general? Have you not hurt your head?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Dost thou mock me?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I mock you not, by heaven.
Would you would bear your fortune like a man!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: A hornèd man’s a monster and a beast.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
There’s many a beast then in a populous city,
And many a civil monster.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Did he confess it?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Good sir, be a man,
Think every bearded fellow that’s but yoked
May draw with you. There’s millions now alive
That nightly lie in those unproper beds
Which they dare swear peculiar. Your case is better.
Oh, ’tis the spite of hell, the fiend’s arch-mock,
To lip a wanton in a secure couch,
And to suppose her chaste. No, let me know,
And knowing what I am, I know what she shall be.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Oh, thou art wise! 'Tis certain.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Stand you awhile apart,
Confine yourself but in a patient list.
Whilst you were here o'erwhelmèd with your grief—
A passion most resulting such a man—
Cassio came hither. I shifted him away
And laid good ’scuses upon your ecstasy,
Bade him anon return and here speak with me,
The which he promised. Do but encave yourself,
And mark the fleers, the gibes, and notable scorns
That dwell in every region of his face.
For I will make him tell the tale anew
Where, how, how oft, how long ago, and when
He hath, and is again to cope your wife.
I say, but mark his gesture. Marry, patience,
Or I shall say you are all in all in spleen,
And nothing of a man.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Dost thou hear, Iago?
I will be found most cunning in my patience,
But—dost thou hear?—most bloody.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
That’s not amiss,
But yet keep time in all. Will you withdraw?
Enter CASSIO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
As he shall smile, Othello shall go mad.
And his unbookish jealousy must construe
Poor Cassio’s smiles, gestures, and light behavior
Quite in the wrong.— How do you now, lieutenant?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
The worser that you give me the addition
Whose want even kills me.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Ply Desdemona well, and you are sure on ’t.
Now if this suit lay in Bianca’s power
How quickly should you speed!

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Alas, poor caitiff!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Look how he laughs already!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: I never knew woman love man so.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Alas, poor rogue, I think indeed she loves me.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Now he denies it faintly, and laughs it out.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Do you hear, Cassio?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Now he importunes him
To tell it o'er. Go to, well said, well said.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
She gives it out that you shall marry her.
Do you intend it?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Ha, ha, ha!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Do ye triumph, Roman? Do you triumph?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
I marry her! What? A customer? Prithee bear some
charity to my wit. Do not think it so unwholesome. Ha,
ha, ha!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: So, so, so, so! They laugh that win!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Faith, the cry goes that you shall marry her.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Prithee say true!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: I am a very villain else.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Have you scored me? Well.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
This is the monkey’s own giving out. She is persuaded I
will marry her, out of her own love and flattery, not
out of my promise.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Iago beckons me. Now he begins the story.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
She was here even now. She haunts me in every place. I
was the other day talking on the sea-bank with certain
Venetians, and thither comes the bauble and, by this
hand, she falls me thus about my neck—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Crying “O dear Cassio!” as it were. His gesture imports
it.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
So hangs and lolls and weeps upon me, so shakes, and
pulls me! Ha, ha, ha!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Now he tells how she plucked him to my chamber. Oh, I
see that nose of yours, but not that dog I shall throw
it to.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Well, I must leave her company.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Before me! Look, where she comes.
Enter BIANCA

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
'Tis such another fitchew. Marry, a perfumed one.—
What do you mean by this haunting of me?

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text:
Let the devil and his dam haunt you! What did you mean
by that same handkerchief you gave me even now? I was a
fine fool to take it. I must take out the work? A likely
piece of work, that you should find it in your chamber,
and not know who left it there! This is some minx’s
token, and I must take out the work? There, give it your
hobby-horse. Wheresoever you had it, I’ll take out no
work on ’t.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: How now, my sweet Bianca! How now, how now?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: By heaven, that should be my handkerchief!

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text:
If you’ll come to supper tonight, you may. If you will
not, come when you are next prepared for.
Exit

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: After her, after her.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: I must, she’ll rail in the street else.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Will you sup there?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Yes, I intend so.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Well, I may chance to see you, for I would very fain
speak with you.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Prithee come, will you?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Go to! Say no more.
Exit CASSIO

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: [advancing] How shall I murder him, Iago?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Did you perceive how he laughed at his vice?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Oh Iago!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: And did you see the handkerchief?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Was that mine?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Yours by this hand. And to see how he prizes the
foolish woman your wife! She gave it him, and he hath
given it his whore.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
I would have him nine years a-killing. A fine woman! A
fair woman! A sweet woman!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Nay, you must forget that.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Ay, let her rot and perish and be damned tonight, for
she shall not live. No, my heart is turned to stone. I
strike it and it hurts my hand. Oh, the world hath not a
sweeter creature, she might lie by an emperor’s side
and command him tasks.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Nay, that’s not your way.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Hang her! I do but say what she is. So delicate with
her needle, an admirable musician. Oh, she will sing the
savageness out of a bear! Of so high and plenteous wit
and invention!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: She’s the worse for all this.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Oh, a thousand thousand times—and then of so gentle a
condition!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Ay, too gentle.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Nay, that’s certain. But yet the pity of it, Iago! O
Iago, the pity of it, Iago!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
If you are so fond over her iniquity, give her patent
to offend, for if it touch not you it comes near nobody.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I will chop her into messes! Cuckold me?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Oh, ’tis foul in her.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: With mine officer!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: That’s fouler.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Get me some poison, Iago, this night. I’ll not
expostulate with her, lest her body and beauty unprovide
my mind again—This night, Iago!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Do it not with poison. Strangle her in her bed, even
the bed she hath contaminated.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Good, good, the justice of it pleases! Very good!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
And for Cassio, let me be his undertaker. You shall
hear more by midnight.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Excellent good.
A trumpet within

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What trumpet is that same?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I warrant something from Venice. 'Tis Lodovico, this,
comes from the duke. See, your wife’s with him.
Enter LODOVICO, DESDEMONA, and attendants

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: Save you, worthy general!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: With all my heart, sir.

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: The duke and senators of Venice greet you.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I kiss the instrument of their pleasures.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: And what’s the news, good cousin Lodovico?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: I am very glad to see you, signior. Welcome to Cyprus.

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: I thank you. How does lieutenant Cassio?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Lives, sir.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Cousin, there’s fall'n between him and my lord
An unkind breach, but you shall make all well.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Are you sure of that?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: My lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: [reads] “This fail you not to do, as you will—”

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text:
He did not call, he’s busy in the paper.
Is there division ’twixt my lord and Cassio?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
A most unhappy one. I would do much
T' atone them, for the love I bear to Cassio.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Fire and brimstone!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: My lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Are you wise?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: What, is he angry?

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text:
Maybe the letter moved him,
For, as I think, they do command him home,
Deputing Cassio in his government.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: By my troth, I am glad on ’t.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Indeed!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: My lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: I am glad to see you mad.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Why, sweet Othello—

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Devil! [strikes her]

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I have not deserved this.

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text:
My lord, this would not be believed in Venice,
Though I should swear I saw ’t. 'Tis very much.
Make her amends, she weeps.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Oh, devil, devil!
If that the earth could teem with woman’s tears,
Each drop she falls would prove a crocodile.
Out of my sight!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I will not stay to offend you.

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text:
Truly, an obedient lady.
I do beseech your lordship, call her back.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Mistress!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: My lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What would you with her, sir?

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: Who, I, my lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Ay, you did wish that I would make her turn.
Sir, she can turn, and turn, and yet go on,
And turn again. And she can weep, sir, weep.
And she’s obedient, as you say, obedient,
Very obedient. (To DESDEMONA) Proceed you in your
tears.
(To LODOVICO) Concerning this, sir— (To DESDEMONA) Oh,
well-painted passion!
(To LODOVICO) I am commanded home. (To DESDEMONA) Get
you away,
I’ll send for you anon. (To LODOVICO) Sir, I obey the
mandate
And will return to Venice. (To DESDEMONA) Hence,
avaunt!
Exit DESDEMONA

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Cassio shall have my place. And, sir, tonight
I do entreat that we may sup together.
You are welcome, sir, to Cyprus. Goats and monkeys!
Exit

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text:
Is this the noble Moor whom our full senate
Call all in all sufficient? Is this the nature
Whom passion could not shake? Whose solid virtue
The shot of accident nor dart of chance
Could neither graze nor pierce?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: He is much changed.

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: Are his wits safe? Is he not light of brain?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
He’s that he is. I may not breathe my censure
What he might be. If what he might he is not,
I would to heaven he were!

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: What? Strike his wife?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
'Faith, that was not so well. Yet would I knew
That stroke would prove the worst!

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text:
Is it his use?
Or did the letters work upon his blood
And new-create his fault?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Alas, alas!
It is not honesty in me to speak
What I have seen and known. You shall observe him,
And his own courses will denote him so
That I may save my speech. Do but go after
And mark how he continues.

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: I am sorry that I am deceived in him.
Exeunt
Enter OTHELLO and EMILIA

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: You have seen nothing then?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Nor ever heard, nor ever did suspect.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Yes, you have seen Cassio and she together.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
But then I saw no harm, and then I heard
Each syllable that breath made up between them.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What, did they never whisper?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Never, my lord.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Nor send you out o' th' way?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Never.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: To fetch her fan, her gloves, her mask, nor nothing?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Never, my lord.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: That’s strange.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
I durst, my lord, to wager she is honest,
Lay down my soul at stake. If you think other
Remove your thought, it doth abuse your bosom.
If any wretch have put this in your head
Let heaven requite it with the serpent’s curse
For if she be not honest, chaste, and true
There’s no man happy. The purest of their wives
Is foul as slander.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Bid her come hither. Go.
Exit EMILIA

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
She says enough, yet she’s a simple bawd
That cannot say as much. This is a subtle whore,
A closet, lock and key, of villainous secrets.
And yet she’ll kneel and pray, I have seen her do ’t.
Enter DESDEMONA with EMILIA

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: My lord, what is your will?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Pray you, chuck, come hither.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: What is your pleasure?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Let me see your eyes.
Look in my face.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: What horrible fancy’s this?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
(to EMILIA) Some of your function, mistress,
Leave procreants alone and shut the door.
Cough or cry “hem” if any body come.
Your mystery, your mystery! Nay, dispatch!
Exit EMILIA

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Upon my knee, what doth your speech import?
I understand a fury in your words,
But not the words.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Why, what art thou?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Your wife, my lord. Your true and loyal wife.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Come, swear it, damn thyself.
Lest, being like one of heaven, the devils themselves
Should fear to seize thee. Therefore be double damned,
Swear thou art honest!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Heaven doth truly know it.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Heaven truly knows that thou art false as hell.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: To whom, my lord? With whom? How am I false?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Ah, Desdemona, away, away, away!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Alas the heavy day, why do you weep?
Am I the motive of these tears, my lord?
If haply you my father do suspect
An instrument of this your calling back,
Lay not your blame on me. If you have lost him,
Why, I have lost him too.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Had it pleased heaven
To try me with affliction, had they rained
All kinds of sores and shames on my bare head,
Steeped me in poverty to the very lips,
Given to captivity me and my utmost hopes,
I should have found in some place of my soul
A drop of patience. But, alas, to make me
The fixèd figure for the time of scorn
To point his slow and moving finger at!
Yet could I bear that too, well, very well.
But there where I have garnered up my heart,
Where either I must live or bear no life,
The fountain from the which my current runs
Or else dries up—to be discarded thence!
Or keep it as a cistern for foul toads
To knot and gender in! Turn thy complexion there,
Patience, thou young and rose-lipped cherubin,—
Ay, there, look grim as hell!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I hope my noble lord esteems me honest.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Oh, ay, as summer flies are in the shambles,
That quicken even with blowing. O thou weed,
Who art so lovely fair and smell’st so sweet
That the sense aches at thee, would thou hadst ne'er
been born!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Alas, what ignorant sin have I committed?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Was this fair paper, this most goodly book,
Made to write “whore” upon? What committed?
Committed? O thou public commoner!
I should make very forges of my cheeks
That would to cinders burn up modesty
Did I but speak thy deeds. What committed?
Heaven stops the nose at it and the moon winks,
The bawdy wind that kisses all it meets
Is hushed within the hollow mine of earth
And will not hear ’t. What committed!
Impudent strumpet!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: By heaven, you do me wrong!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Are you not a strumpet?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
No, as I am a Christian.
If to preserve this vessel for my lord
From any other foul unlawful touch
Be not to be a strumpet, I am none.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: What, not a whore?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: No, as I shall be saved.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Is ’t possible?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Oh, heaven forgive us!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
I cry you mercy, then,
I took you for that cunning whore of Venice
That married with Othello.— You, mistress,
That have the office opposite to Saint Peter
And keep the gate of hell!
Enter EMILIA

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
You, you, ay, you!
We have done our course. There’s money for your pains.
I pray you, turn the key and keep our counsel.
Exit

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
Alas, what does this gentleman conceive?
How do you, madam? How do you, my good lady?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Faith, half asleep.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Good madam, what’s the matter with my lord?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: With who?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Why, with my lord, madam.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Who is thy lord?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: He that is yours, sweet lady.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
I have none. Do not talk to me, Emilia.
I cannot weep, nor answers have I none,
But what should go by water. Prithee, tonight
Lay on my bed my wedding sheets. Remember,
And call thy husband hither.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Here’s a change indeed!
Exit

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
'Tis meet I should be used so, very meet.
How have I been behaved that he might stick
The small’st opinion on my least misuse?
Enter EMILIA with IAGO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: What is your pleasure, madam? How is ’t with you?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
I cannot tell. Those that do teach young babes
Do it with gentle means and easy tasks.
He might have chid me so, for, in good faith,
I am a child to chiding.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: What is the matter, lady?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
Alas, Iago, my lord hath so bewhored her,
Thrown such despite and heavy terms upon her,
That true hearts cannot bear it.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Am I that name, Iago?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: What name, fair lady?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Such as she says my lord did say I was.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
He called her “whore.” A beggar in his drink
Could not have laid such terms upon his callet.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Why did he so?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I do not know. I am sure I am none such.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Do not weep, do not weep. Alas the day!

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
Hath she forsook so many noble matches,
Her father and her country, and her friends,
To be called “whore”? Would it not make one weep?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: It is my wretched fortune.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Beshrew him for ’t!
How comes this trick upon him?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Nay, heaven doth know.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
I will be hanged, if some eternal villain,
Some busy and insinuating rogue,
Some cogging, cozening slave, to get some office,
Have not devised this slander. I will be hanged else!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Fie, there is no such man. It is impossible.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: If any such there be, heaven pardon him!

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
A halter pardon him and hell gnaw his bones!
Why should he call her “whore?” Who keeps her company?
What place? What time? What form? What likelihood?
The Moor’s abused by some most villainous knave,
Some base notorious knave, some scurvy fellow.
O heavens, that such companions thou’dst unfold,
And put in every honest hand a whip
To lash the rascals naked through the world
Even from the east to th' west!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Speak within door.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
Oh, fie upon them! Some such squire he was
That turned your wit the seamy side without
And made you to suspect me with the Moor.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: You are a fool. Go to.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Alas Iago,
What shall I do to win my lord again?
Good friend, go to him. For, by this light of heaven,
I know not how I lost him. Here I kneel:
If e'er my will did trespass 'gainst his love,
Either in discourse of thought or actual deed,
Or that mine eyes, mine ears, or any sense,
Delighted them, or any other form,
Or that I do not yet, and ever did,
And ever will— though he do shake me off
To beggarly divorcement—love him dearly,
Comfort forswear me! Unkindness may do much,
And his unkindness may defeat my life,
But never taint my love. I cannot say “whore,”
It does abhor me now I speak the word.
To do the act that might the addition earn
Not the world’s mass of vanity could make me.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I pray you, be content, ’tis but his humor.
The business of the state does him offence,
And he does chide with you.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: If ’twere no other—

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: 'Tis but so, I warrant.
Trumpets sound

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Hark, how these instruments summon to supper.
The messengers of Venice stays the meat.
Go in, and weep not. All things shall be well.
Exeunt DESDEMONA and EMILIA
Enter RODERIGO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: How now, Roderigo!

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: I do not find that thou deal’st justly with me.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: What in the contrary?

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text:
Every day thou daff’st me with some device, Iago, and
rather, as it seems to me now, keep’st from me all
conveniency than suppliest me with the least advantage
of hope. I will indeed no longer endure it, nor am I yet
persuaded to put up in peace what already I have
foolishly suffered.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Will you hear me, Roderigo?

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text:
I have heard too much, and your words and performances
are no kin together.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: You charge me most unjustly.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text:
With naught but truth. I have wasted myself out of my
means. The jewels you have had from me to deliver
Desdemona would half have corrupted a votaress. You have
told me she hath received them and returned me
expectations and comforts of sudden respect and
acquaintance, but I find none.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Well, go to. Very well.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text:
“Very well,” “go to”! I cannot go to, man, nor ’tis
not very well. Nay, I think it is scurvy, and begin to
find myself fopped in it.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Very well.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text:
I tell you ’tis not very well. I will make myself known
to Desdemona. If she will return me my jewels I will
give over my suit and repent my unlawful solicitation.
If not, assure yourself I will seek satisfaction of you.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: You have said now.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text:
Ay, and said nothing but what I protest intendment of
doing.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Why, now I see there’s mettle in thee, and even from
this instant to build on thee a better opinion than ever
before. Give me thy hand, Roderigo. Thou hast taken
against me a most just exception, but yet I protest I
have dealt most directly in thy affair.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: It hath not appeared.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
I grant indeed it hath not appeared, and your suspicion
is not without wit and judgment. But, Roderigo, if thou
hast that in thee indeed, which I have greater reason
to believe now than ever—I mean purpose, courage and
valor—this night show it. If thou the next night
following enjoy not Desdemona, take me from this world
with treachery and devise engines for my life.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: Well, what is it? Is it within reason and compass?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Sir, there is especial commission come from Venice to
depute Cassio in Othello’s place.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text:
Is that true? Why, then Othello and Desdemona return
again to Venice.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Oh, no, he goes into Mauritania and taketh away with
him the fair Desdemona, unless his abode be lingered
here by some accident—wherein none can be so determinate
as the removing of Cassio.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: How do you mean, removing of him?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Why, by making him uncapable of Othello’s place:
knocking out his brains.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: And that you would have me to do!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Ay, if you dare do yourself a profit and a right. He
sups tonight with a harlotry, and thither will I go to
him. He knows not yet of his honorable fortune. If you
will watch his going thence (which I will fashion to
fall out between twelve and one) you may take him at
your pleasure. I will be near to second your attempt,
and he shall fall between us. Come, stand not amazed at
it, but go along with me. I will show you such a
necessity in his death that you shall think yourself
bound to put it on him. It is now high suppertime, and
the night grows to waste. About it!

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: I will hear further reason for this.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: And you shall be satisfied.
Exeunt
Enter OTHELLO, LODOVICO, DESDEMONA, EMILIA and attendants

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: I do beseech you, sir, trouble yourself no further.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Oh, pardon me, ’twill do me good to walk.

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: Madam, good night. I humbly thank your ladyship.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Your honor is most welcome.

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Will you walk, sir?—O Desdemona—

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: My lord?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
Get you to bed on th' instant, I will be returned
Forthwith. Dismiss your attendant there, look ’t be
done.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I will, my lord.
Exeunt OTHELLO, LODOVICO, and attendants

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: How goes it now? He looks gentler than he did.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
He says he will return incontinent,
And hath commanded me to go to bed
And bid me to dismiss you.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Dismiss me?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
It was his bidding. Therefore, good Emilia,
Give me my nightly wearing, and adieu.
We must not now displease him.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Ay. Would you had never seen him!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
So would not I. My love doth so approve him
That even his stubbornness, his checks, his frowns—
Prithee, unpin me—have grace and favor.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: I have laid those sheets you bade me on the bed.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
All’s one. Good Father, how foolish are our minds!
If I do die before thee, prithee, shroud me
In one of these same sheets.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Come, come! You talk!

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
My mother had a maid called Barbary,
She was in love, and he she loved proved mad
And did forsake her. She had a song of “Willow,”
An old thing ’twas, but it expressed her fortune
And she died singing it. That song tonight
Will not go from my mind. I have much to do
But to go hang my head all at one side
And sing it like poor Barbary. Prithee, dispatch.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Shall I go fetch your nightgown?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
No, unpin me here.
This Lodovico is a proper man.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: A very handsome man.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: He speaks well.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
I know a lady in Venice would have walked barefoot to
Palestine for a touch of his nether lip.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
( singing)
The poor soul sat sighing by a sycamore tree,
Sing all a green willow.
Her hand on her bosom, her head on her knee,
Sing willow, willow, willow.
The fresh streams ran by her, and murmured her moans,
Sing willow, willow, willow.
Her salt tears fell from her, and softened the stones
Sing willow, willow, willow—
Lay by these—
Willow, willow—
Prithee, hie thee, he’ll come anon—
Sing all a green willow must be my garland.
Let nobody blame him, his scorn I approve—
Nay, that’s not next—Hark! Who is ’t that knocks?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: It’s the wind.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
(sings)
I called my love false love but what said he then?
Sing willow, willow, willow.
If I court more women you’ll couch with more men—
So, get thee gone, good night. Mine eyes do itch,
Doth that bode weeping?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: 'Tis neither here nor there.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
I have heard it said so. Oh, these men, these men!
Dost thou in conscience think—tell me, Emilia—
That there be women do abuse their husbands
In such gross kind?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: There be some such, no question.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Wouldst thou do such a deed for all the world?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Why, would not you?

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: No, by this heavenly light!

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
Nor I neither, by this heavenly light.
I might do ’t as well i' th' dark.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Wouldst thou do such a deed for all the world?

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
The world’s a huge thing. It is a great price for a
small vice.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: In troth, I think thou wouldst not.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
In troth, I think I should, and undo ’t when I had
done. Marry, I would not do such a thing for a
joint-ring, nor for measures of lawn, nor for gowns,
petticoats, nor caps, nor any petty exhibition. But for
the whole world? Why, who would not make her husband a
cuckold to make him a monarch? I should venture
purgatory for ’t.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Beshrew me, if I would do such a wrong
For the whole world.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
Why the wrong is but a wrong i' th' world, and having
the world for your labor, ’tis a wrong in your own
world, and you might quickly make it right.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: I do not think there is any such woman.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text:
Yes, a dozen, and as many to th' vantage as would store
the world they played for.
But I do think it is their husbands' faults
If wives do fall. Say that they slack their duties
And pour our treasures into foreign laps,
Or else break out in peevish jealousies,
Throwing restraint upon us. Or say they strike us,
Or scant our former having in despite.
Why, we have galls, and though we have some grace,
Yet have we some revenge. Let husbands know
Their wives have sense like them. They see and smell
And have their palates both for sweet and sour,
As husbands have. What is it that they do
When they change us for others? Is it sport?
I think it is. And doth affection breed it?
I think it doth. Is ’t frailty that thus errs?
It is so too. And have not we affections,
Desires for sport, and frailty, as men have?
Then let them use us well, else let them know,
The ills we do, their ills instruct us so.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text:
Good night, good night. Heaven me such uses send,
Not to pick bad from bad, but by bad mend!
Exeunt
Enter IAGO and RODERIGO

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Here, stand behind this bulk, straight will he come.
Wear thy good rapier bare, and put it home.
Quick, quick! Fear nothing. I’ll be at thy elbow.
It makes us, or it mars us. Think on that,
And fix most firm thy resolution.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: Be near at hand, I may miscarry in ’t.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Here, at thy hand. Be bold, and take thy stand.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text:
I have no great devotion to the deed
And yet he hath given me satisfying reasons.
'Tis but a man gone. Forth, my sword: he dies.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
(aside) I have rubbed this young quat almost to the
sense,
And he grows angry. Now, whether he kill Cassio
Or Cassio him, or each do kill the other,
Every way makes my gain. Live Roderigo,
He calls me to a restitution large
Of gold and jewels that I bobbed from him
As gifts to Desdemona.
It must not be. If Cassio do remain
He hath a daily beauty in his life
That makes me ugly. And besides, the Moor
May unfold me to him—there stand I in much peril.
No, he must die. But so, I hear him coming.
Enter CASSIO

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: I know his gait, ’tis he.—Villain, thou diest!

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
That thrust had been mine enemy indeed
But that my coat is better than thou know’st
I will make proof of thine.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: Oh, I am slain!
IAGO from behind wounds CASSIO in the legs, exits

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: I am maimed for ever. Help, ho! Murder! Murder!
Enter OTHELLO

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: The voice of Cassio. Iago keeps his word.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: Oh, villain that I am!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: It is even so.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Oh, help, ho! Light! A surgeon!

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
'Tis he. Oh, brave Iago, honest and just,
That hast such noble sense of thy friend’s wrong!
Thou teachest me. Minion, your dear lies dead,
And your unblest fate hies. Strumpet, I come.
For, of my heart, those charms, thine eyes, are
blotted.
Thy bed, lust-stained, shall with lust’s blood be
spotted.
Exit
Enter LODOVICO and GRATIANO

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: What, ho! No watch? No passage? Murder! Murder!

units.speaker: GRATIANO
units.text: 'Tis some mischance, the cry is very direful.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Oh, help!

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: Hark!

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: Oh, wretched villain!

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text:
Two or three groan. 'Tis heavy night,
These may be counterfeits. Let’s think ’t unsafe
To come in to the cry without more help.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: Nobody come? Then shall I bleed to death.

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: Hark!
Enter IAGO

units.speaker: GRATIANO
units.text: Here’s one comes in his shirt, with light and weapons.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Who’s there? Whose noise is this that ones on murder?

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: We do not know.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Do not you hear a cry?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: Here, here! For heaven’s sake, help me!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: What’s the matter?

units.speaker: GRATIANO
units.text: [To LODOVICO] This is Othello’s ancient, as I take it.

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: The same indeed, a very valiant fellow.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: [To CASSIO] What are you here that cry so grievously?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
Iago? Oh, I am spoiled, undone by villains!
Give me some help.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Oh, me, lieutenant! What villains have done this?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text:
I think that one of them is hereabout,
And cannot make away.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Oh, treacherous villains!—
[To LODOVICO and GRATIANO]
What are you there? Come in, and give some help.

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: Oh, help me there!

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: That’s one of them.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: O murd'rous slave! O villain!

units.speaker: RODERIGO
units.text: O damned Iago! O inhuman dog!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Kill men i' th' dark! Where be these bloody thieves?
How silent is this town!— Ho! murder! murder!—
What may you be? Are you of good or evil?

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: As you shall prove us, praise us.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Signior Lodovico?

units.speaker: LODOVICO
units.text: He, sir.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: I cry you mercy. Here’s Cassio hurt by villains.

units.speaker: GRATIANO
units.text: Cassio!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: How is ’t, brother!

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: My leg is cut in two.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Marry, heaven forbid!
Light, gentlemen, I’ll bind it with my shirt.
Enter BIANCA

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: What is the matter, ho? Who is ’t that cried?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Who is ’t that cried?

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text:
Oh, my dear Cassio!
My sweet Cassio! O Cassio, Cassio, Cassio!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
O notable strumpet! Cassio, may you suspect
Who they should be that have thus mangled you?

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: No.

units.speaker: GRATIANO
units.text: I am sorry to find you thus. I have been to seek you.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Lend me a garter. So.—Oh, for a chair,
To bear him easily hence!

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: Alas, he faints! O Cassio, Cassio, Cassio!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Gentlemen all, I do suspect this trash
To be a party in this injury.—
Patience awhile, good Cassio.— Come, come,
Lend me a light. Know we this face or no?
Alas, my friend and my dear countryman
Roderigo! No—yes, sure! Yes, ’tis Roderigo.

units.speaker: GRATIANO
units.text: What, of Venice?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Even he, sir. Did you know him?

units.speaker: GRATIANO
units.text: Know him? Ay.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Signior Gratiano? I cry you gentle pardon,
These bloody accidents must excuse my manners
That so neglected you.

units.speaker: GRATIANO
units.text: I am glad to see you.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: How do you, Cassio?—Oh, a chair, a chair!

units.speaker: GRATIANO
units.text: Roderigo!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: He, he, ’tis he.

units.speaker: CASSIO
units.text: None in the world, nor do I know the man.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
[To BIANCA]
What, look you pale?—Oh, bear him out o' the air.—
Enter EMILIA

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Alas, what is the matter? What is the matter, husband?

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Cassio hath here been set on in the dark
By Roderigo and fellows that are ’scaped.
He’s almost slain, and Roderigo dead.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Alas, good gentleman! Alas, good Cassio!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
This is the fruits of whoring. Prithee, Emilia,
Go know of Cassio where he supped tonight.—
[To BIANCA] What, do you shake at that?

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text: He supped at my house, but I therefore shake not.

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text: Oh, did he so? I charge you, go with me.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: Oh, fie upon thee, strumpet!

units.speaker: BIANCA
units.text:
I am no strumpet, but of life as honest
As you that thus abuse me.

units.speaker: EMILIA
units.text: As I! Fie upon thee!

units.speaker: IAGO
units.text:
Kind gentlemen, let’s go see poor Cassio dressed.—
Come, mistress, you must tell ’s another tale.
Emilia, run you to the citadel
And tell my lord and lady what hath happed.—
Will you go on afore?
(aside)
This is the night
That either makes me or fordoes me quite.
Exeunt
DESDEMONA asleep in bed Enter OTHELLO with a light

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text:
It is the cause, it is the cause, my soul.
Let me not name it to you, you chaste stars,
It is the cause. Yet I’ll not shed her blood,
Nor scar that whiter skin of hers than snow
And smooth as monumental alabaster.
Yet she must die, else she’ll betray more men.
Put out the light, and then put out the light.
If I quench thee, thou flaming minister,
I can again thy former light restore
Should I repent me. But once put out thy light,
Thou cunning’st pattern of excelling nature,
I know not where is that Promethean heat
That can thy light relume. When I have plucked thy rose
I cannot give it vital growth again,
It must needs wither. I’ll smell thee on the tree.
Oh, balmy breath, that dost almost persuade
Justice to break her sword! One more, one more.
Be thus when thou art dead and I will kill thee
And love thee after. (kissing her) One more, and that’s
the last.
So sweet was ne'er so fatal. I must weep,
But they are cruel tears. This sorrow’s heavenly,
It strikes where it doth love. She wakes.

units.speaker: DESDEMONA
units.text: Who’s there? Othello?

units.speaker: OTHELLO
units.text: Ay, Desdemona.
