sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: I am your own for ever.
Exeunt
Enter DESDEMONA, EMILIA, and CLOWN

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Do you know, sirrah, where Lieutenant Cassio lies?

sentences.speaker: CLOWN
sentences.text: I dare not say he lies anywhere.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Why, man?

sentences.speaker: CLOWN
sentences.text:
He’s a soldier, and for one to say a soldier lies,
’tis stabbing.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Go to. Where lodges he?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Go to. Where lodges he?

sentences.speaker: CLOWN
sentences.text: To tell you where he lodges is to tell you where I lie.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Can anything be made of this?

sentences.speaker: CLOWN
sentences.text:
I know not where he lodges, and for me to devise a
lodging and say he lies here, or he lies there, were to
lie in mine own throat.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Can you inquire him out and be edified by report?

sentences.speaker: CLOWN
sentences.text:
I will catechize the world for him, that is, make
questions, and by them answer.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Seek him, bid him come hither. Tell him I have moved my
lord on his behalf, and hope all will be well.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Seek him, bid him come hither. Tell him I have moved my
lord on his behalf, and hope all will be well.

sentences.speaker: CLOWN
sentences.text:
To do this is within the compass of man’s wit, and
therefore I will attempt the doing it.

sentences.speaker: CLOWN
sentences.text:
To do this is within the compass of man’s wit, and
therefore I will attempt the doing it.
Exit

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Where should I lose that handkerchief, Emilia?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: I know not, madam.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Believe me, I had rather have lost my purse
Full of crusadoes. And but my noble Moor
Is true of mind and made of no such baseness
As jealous creatures are, it were enough
To put him to ill thinking.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Believe me, I had rather have lost my purse
Full of crusadoes. And but my noble Moor
Is true of mind and made of no such baseness
As jealous creatures are, it were enough
To put him to ill thinking.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Is he not jealous?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Who, he? I think the sun where he was born
Drew all such humors from him.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Who, he? I think the sun where he was born
Drew all such humors from him.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Look where he comes.
Enter OTHELLO

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
I will not leave him now till Cassio
Be called to him.—How is ’t with you, my lord?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Well, my good lady.— [aside] Oh, hardness to dissemble!—
How do you, Desdemona?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Well, my good lord.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Give me your hand. This hand is moist, my lady.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Give me your hand. This hand is moist, my lady.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: It hath felt no age nor known no sorrow.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
This argues fruitfulness and liberal heart.
Hot, hot, and moist. This hand of yours requires
A sequester from liberty, fasting, and prayer,
Much castigation, exercise devout,
For here’s a young and sweating devil here,
That commonly rebels. 'Tis a good hand,
A frank one.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
You may indeed say so,
For ’twas that hand that gave away my heart.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
A liberal hand. The hearts of old gave hands,
But our new heraldry is hands, not hearts.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
A liberal hand. The hearts of old gave hands,
But our new heraldry is hands, not hearts.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
A liberal hand. The hearts of old gave hands,
But our new heraldry is hands, not hearts.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I cannot speak of this. Come now, your promise.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I cannot speak of this. Come now, your promise.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What promise, chuck?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I have sent to bid Cassio come speak with you.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I have a salt and sorry rheum offends me.
Lend me thy handkerchief.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I have a salt and sorry rheum offends me.
Lend me thy handkerchief.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Here, my lord.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: That which I gave you.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I have it not about me.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Not?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: No, indeed, my lord.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
That’s a fault. That handkerchief
Did an Egyptian to my mother give,
She was a charmer and could almost read
The thoughts of people. She told her, while she kept it
'Twould make her amiable and subdue my father
Entirely to her love, but if she lost it
Or made gift of it, my father’s eye
Should hold her loathèd and his spirits should hunt
After new fancies. She, dying, gave it me
And bid me, when my fate would have me wived,
To give it her. I did so, and take heed on ’t,
Make it a darling like your precious eye.
To lose ’t or give ’t away were such perdition
As nothing else could match.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Is ’t possible?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
'Tis true. There’s magic in the web of it.
A sibyl, that had numbered in the world
The sun to course two hundred compasses,
In her prophetic fury sewed the work.
The worms were hallowed that did breed the silk,
And it was dyed in mummy which the skillful
Conserved of maidens' hearts.

sentences.text:

And it was dyed in mummy which the skillful
Conserved of maidens' hearts.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
'Tis true. There’s magic in the web of it.
A sibyl, that had numbered in the world
The sun to course two hundred compasses,
In her prophetic fury sewed the work.
The worms were hallowed that did breed the silk,
And it was dyed in mummy which the skillful
Conserved of maidens' hearts.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Indeed? Is ’t true?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Indeed? Is ’t true?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Most veritable, therefore look to ’t well.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Then would to Heaven that I had never seen ’t!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Ha! Wherefore?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Ha! Wherefore?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Why do you speak so startingly and rash?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Is ’t lost? Is ’t gone? Speak, is ’t out o' th' way?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: How!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO

I am most unhappy in the loss of it.

'Tis not a year or two shows us a man.
They are all but stomachs, and we all but food.
To eat us hungerly, and when they are full,
They belch us. Look you, Cassio and my husband!

There is no other way. 'Tis she must do ’t,
And, lo, the happiness! Go and importune her.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: How now, good Cassio, what’s the news with you?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: How now, good Cassio, what’s the news with you?

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text:
Madam, my former suit. I do beseech you
That by your virtuous means I may again
Exist, and be a member of his love
Whom I, with all the office of my heart
Entirely honor. I would not be delayed.
If my offence be of such mortal kind
That nor my service past, nor present sorrows,
Nor purposed merit in futurity,
Can ransom me into his love again,
But to know so must be my benefit.
So shall I clothe me in a forced content,
And shut myself up in some other course,
To fortune’s alms.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Alas, thrice-gentle Cassio,
My advocation is not now in tune.
My lord is not my lord, nor should I know him
Were he in favor as in humor altered.
So help me every spirit sanctified
As I have spoken for you all my best
And stood within the blank of his displeasure
For my free speech. You must awhile be patient.
What I can do I will, and more I will
Than for myself I dare. Let that suffice you.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Is my lord angry?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
He went hence but now,
And certainly in strange unquietness.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Can he be angry? I have seen the cannon
When it hath blown his ranks into the air
And, like the devil, from his very arm
Puffed his own brother— and is he angry?
Something of moment then, I will go meet him.
There’s matter in ’t indeed, if he be angry.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I prithee, do so.
Exit IAGO

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Something, sure, of state,
Either from Venice, or some unhatched practice
Made demonstrable here in Cyprus to him,
Hath puddled his clear spirit, and in such cases
Men’s natures wrangle with inferior things,
Though great ones are their object. 'Tis even so,
For let our finger ache and it endues
Our other healthful members even to that sense
Of pain. Nay, we must think men are not gods,
Nor of them look for such observances
As fit the bridal. Beshrew me much, Emilia,
I was, unhandsome warrior as I am,
Arraigning his unkindness with my soul,
But now I find I had suborned the witness,
And he’s indicted falsely.

Pray heaven it be
State matters, as you think, and no conception
Nor no jealous toy concerning you.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Alas the day! I never gave him cause.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
But jealous souls will not be answered so.
They are not ever jealous for the cause,
But jealous for they’re jealous. It is a monster
Begot upon itself, born on itself.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA

I will go seek him.—Cassio, walk hereabout.
If I do find him fit, I’ll move your suit
And seek to effect it to my uttermost.

Enter BIANCA

sentences.speaker: BIANCA
sentences.text: Save you, friend Cassio!

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text:
What make you from home?
How is ’t with you, my most fair Bianca?
Indeed, sweet love, I was coming to your house.

sentences.speaker: BIANCA

What, keep a week away? Seven days and nights?
Eight score eight hours? And lovers' absent hours
More tedious than the dial eightscore times!
Oh weary reckoning!

Pardon me, Bianca,
I have this while with leaden thoughts been pressed,
But I shall, in a more continuate time,
Strike off this score of absence. Sweet Bianca,
(giving her DESDEMONA’s handkerchief)
Take me this work out.

Is ’t come to this? Well, well.

I like the work well. Ere it be demanded,
As like enough it will, I would have it copied.
Take it and do ’t, and leave me for this time.

sentences.speaker: BIANCA
sentences.text: Leave you! Wherefore?

sentences.speaker: BIANCA
sentences.text: Leave you! Wherefore?

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text:
I do attend here on the general
And think it no addition, nor my wish,
To have him see me womaned.

sentences.speaker: BIANCA
sentences.text: Why, I pray you?

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text: Not that I love you not.

sentences.speaker: BIANCA
sentences.text:
But that you do not love me.
I pray you bring me on the way a little
And say if I shall see you soon at night.

sentences.speaker: BIANCA
sentences.text:
But that you do not love me.
I pray you bring me on the way a little
And say if I shall see you soon at night.

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text:
'Tis but a little way that I can bring you,
For I attend here. But I’ll see you soon.

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text:
'Tis but a little way that I can bring you,
For I attend here. But I’ll see you soon.

sentences.speaker: BIANCA
sentences.text: 'Tis very good. I must be circumstanced.

sentences.speaker: BIANCA
sentences.text: 'Tis very good. I must be circumstanced.
Exeunt
Enter OTHELLO and IAGO

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Will you think so?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Think so, Iago?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
What,
To kiss in private?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: An unauthorized kiss!

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Or to be naked with her friend in bed
An hour or more, not meaning any harm?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Naked in bed, Iago, and not mean harm!
It is hypocrisy against the devil.
They that mean virtuously, and yet do so,
The devil their virtue tempts, and they tempt heaven.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Naked in bed, Iago, and not mean harm!
It is hypocrisy against the devil.
They that mean virtuously, and yet do so,
The devil their virtue tempts, and they tempt heaven.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Naked in bed, Iago, and not mean harm!
It is hypocrisy against the devil.
They that mean virtuously, and yet do so,
The devil their virtue tempts, and they tempt heaven.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
So they do nothing, ’tis a venial slip.
But if I give my wife a handkerchief—

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
So they do nothing, ’tis a venial slip.
But if I give my wife a handkerchief—

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What then?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Why then ’tis hers, my lord, and, being hers,
She may, I think, bestow ’t on any man.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Why then ’tis hers, my lord, and, being hers,
She may, I think, bestow ’t on any man.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
She is protectress of her honor too.
May she give that?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
She is protectress of her honor too.
May she give that?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Her honor is an essence that’s not seen,
They have it very oft that have it not.
But for the handkerchief—

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Her honor is an essence that’s not seen,
They have it very oft that have it not.
But for the handkerchief—

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Her honor is an essence that’s not seen,
They have it very oft that have it not.
But for the handkerchief—

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
By heaven, I would most gladly have forgot it.
Thou saidst—Oh, it comes o'er my memory,
As doth the raven o'er the infectious house,
Boding to all—he had my handkerchief.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
By heaven, I would most gladly have forgot it.
Thou saidst—Oh, it comes o'er my memory,
As doth the raven o'er the infectious house,
Boding to all—he had my handkerchief.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Ay, what of that?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: That’s not so good now.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
What if I had said I had seen him do you wrong?
Or heard him say—as knaves be such abroad,
Who having, by their own importunate suit,
Or voluntary dotage of some mistress,
Convincèd or supplied them, cannot choose
But they must blab—

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
What if I had said I had seen him do you wrong?
Or heard him say—as knaves be such abroad,
Who having, by their own importunate suit,
Or voluntary dotage of some mistress,
Convincèd or supplied them, cannot choose
But they must blab—

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Hath he said any thing?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
He hath, my lord, but be you well assured
No more than he’ll unswear.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What hath he said?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Why, that he did—I know not what he did.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What? what?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What? what?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Lie—

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: With her?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: With her, on her, what you will.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Lie with her? lie on her? We say “lie on her” when they
belie her! Lie with her—that’s fulsome.
Handkerchief—confessions—handkerchief! To confess, and
be hanged for his labor. First to be hanged, and then to
confess—I tremble at it . Nature would not invest
herself in such shadowing passion without some
instruction. It is not words that shake me thus. Pish!
Noses, ears, and lips. Is ’t possible?
Confess!—Handkerchief!—Oh, devil!—

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Work on, My medicine, work! Thus credulous fools are
caught,
And many worthy and chaste dames even thus,
All guiltless, meet reproach.— What, ho! My lord!
My lord, I say! Othello!

Enter CASSIO

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: How now, Cassio!

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text: What’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
My lord is fall'n into an epilepsy.
This is his second fit. He had one yesterday.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
My lord is fall'n into an epilepsy.
This is his second fit. He had one yesterday.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
My lord is fall'n into an epilepsy.
This is his second fit. He had one yesterday.

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text: Rub him about the temples.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
No, forbear.
The lethargy must have his quiet course.
If not, he foams at mouth and by and by
Breaks out to savage madness. Look, he stirs.
Do you withdraw yourself a little while,
He will recover straight. When he is gone
I would on great occasion speak with you.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: How is it, general? Have you not hurt your head?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: How is it, general? Have you not hurt your head?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Dost thou mock me?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
I mock you not, by heaven.
Would you would bear your fortune like a man!

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
I mock you not, by heaven.
Would you would bear your fortune like a man!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: A hornèd man’s a monster and a beast.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
There’s many a beast then in a populous city,
And many a civil monster.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Did he confess it?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Good sir, be a man,
Think every bearded fellow that’s but yoked
May draw with you. There’s millions now alive
That nightly lie in those unproper beds
Which they dare swear peculiar. Your case is better.
Oh, ’tis the spite of hell, the fiend’s arch-mock,
To lip a wanton in a secure couch,
And to suppose her chaste. No, let me know,
And knowing what I am, I know what she shall be.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Oh, thou art wise! 'Tis certain.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Stand you awhile apart,
Confine yourself but in a patient list.
Whilst you were here o'erwhelmèd with your grief—
A passion most resulting such a man—
Cassio came hither. I shifted him away
And laid good ’scuses upon your ecstasy,
Bade him anon return and here speak with me,
The which he promised. Do but encave yourself,
And mark the fleers, the gibes, and notable scorns
That dwell in every region of his face.
For I will make him tell the tale anew
Where, how, how oft, how long ago, and when
He hath, and is again to cope your wife.
I say, but mark his gesture. Marry, patience,
Or I shall say you are all in all in spleen,
And nothing of a man.

He hath, and is again to cope your wife.
I say, but mark his gesture. Marry, patience,
Or I shall say you are all in all in spleen,
And nothing of a man.

sentences.speaker: BIANCA
sentences.text:
Let the devil and his dam haunt you! What did you mean
by that same handkerchief you gave me even now? I was a
fine fool to take it. I must take out the work? A likely
piece of work, that you should find it in your chamber,
and not know who left it there! This is some minx’s
token, and I must take out the work? There, give it your
hobby-horse. Wheresoever you had it, I’ll take out no
work on ’t.

and command him tasks.

Make her amends, she weeps.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Oh, devil, devil!
If that the earth could teem with woman’s tears,
Each drop she falls would prove a crocodile.
Out of my sight!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Oh, devil, devil!
If that the earth could teem with woman’s tears,
Each drop she falls would prove a crocodile.
Out of my sight!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I will not stay to offend you.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
Truly, an obedient lady.
I do beseech your lordship, call her back.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
Truly, an obedient lady.
I do beseech your lordship, call her back.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Mistress!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: My lord?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What would you with her, sir?

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Who, I, my lord?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Ay, you did wish that I would make her turn.
Sir, she can turn, and turn, and yet go on,
And turn again. And she can weep, sir, weep.
And she’s obedient, as you say, obedient,
Very obedient. (To DESDEMONA) Proceed you in your
tears.
(To LODOVICO) Concerning this, sir— (To DESDEMONA) Oh,
well-painted passion!
(To LODOVICO) I am commanded home. (To DESDEMONA) Get
you away,
I’ll send for you anon. (To LODOVICO) Sir, I obey the
mandate
And will return to Venice. (To DESDEMONA) Hence,
avaunt!

sentences.text:
Cassio shall have my place. And, sir, tonight
I do entreat that we may sup together.
You are welcome, sir, to Cyprus. Goats and monkeys!

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
Is this the noble Moor whom our full senate
Call all in all sufficient? Is this the nature
Whom passion could not shake? Whose solid virtue
The shot of accident nor dart of chance
Could neither graze nor pierce?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: He is much changed.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Are his wits safe? Is he not light of brain?

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Are his wits safe? Is he not light of brain?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
He’s that he is. I may not breathe my censure
What he might be. If what he might he is not,
I would to heaven he were!

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:

What he might be. If what he might he is not,
I would to heaven he were!

sentences.speaker: IAGO

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: What? Strike his wife?

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: What? Strike his wife?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
'Faith, that was not so well. Yet would I knew
That stroke would prove the worst!

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
'Faith, that was not so well. Yet would I knew
That stroke would prove the worst!

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
Is it his use?
Or did the letters work upon his blood
And new-create his fault?

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
Is it his use?
Or did the letters work upon his blood
And new-create his fault?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Alas, alas!
It is not honesty in me to speak
What I have seen and known. You shall observe him,
And his own courses will denote him so
That I may save my speech. Do but go after
And mark how he continues.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: I am sorry that I am deceived in him.
Exeunt
Enter OTHELLO and EMILIA

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: You have seen nothing then?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Nor ever heard, nor ever did suspect.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Yes, you have seen Cassio and she together.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
But then I saw no harm, and then I heard
Each syllable that breath made up between them.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What, did they never whisper?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Never, my lord.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Nor send you out o' th' way?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Never.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: To fetch her fan, her gloves, her mask, nor nothing?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: To fetch her fan, her gloves, her mask, nor nothing?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Never, my lord.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: That’s strange.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
I durst, my lord, to wager she is honest,
Lay down my soul at stake. If you think other
Remove your thought, it doth abuse your bosom.
If any wretch have put this in your head
Let heaven requite it with the serpent’s curse
For if she be not honest, chaste, and true
There’s no man happy. The purest of their wives
Is foul as slander.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
She says enough, yet she’s a simple bawd
That cannot say as much. This is a subtle whore,
A closet, lock and key, of villainous secrets.
And yet she’ll kneel and pray, I have seen her do ’t.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: My lord, what is your will?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Pray you, chuck, come hither.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: What is your pleasure?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Let me see your eyes.
Look in my face.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Let me see your eyes.
Look in my face.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: What horrible fancy’s this?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
(to EMILIA) Some of your function, mistress,
Leave procreants alone and shut the door.
Cough or cry “hem” if any body come.
Your mystery, your mystery! Nay, dispatch!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Upon my knee, what doth your speech import?
I understand a fury in your words,
But not the words.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Upon my knee, what doth your speech import?
I understand a fury in your words,
But not the words.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Why, what art thou?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Your wife, my lord. Your true and loyal wife.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Your wife, my lord. Your true and loyal wife.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Come, swear it, damn thyself.
Lest, being like one of heaven, the devils themselves
Should fear to seize thee. Therefore be double damned,
Swear thou art honest!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Come, swear it, damn thyself.
Lest, being like one of heaven, the devils themselves

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Heaven doth truly know it.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Heaven truly knows that thou art false as hell.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: To whom, my lord? With whom? How am I false?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: To whom, my lord? With whom? How am I false?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: To whom, my lord? With whom? How am I false?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Ah, Desdemona, away, away, away!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Alas the heavy day, why do you weep?
Am I the motive of these tears, my lord?
If haply you my father do suspect
An instrument of this your calling back,
Lay not your blame on me. If you have lost him,
Why, I have lost him too.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Had it pleased heaven
To try me with affliction, had they rained
All kinds of sores and shames on my bare head,
Steeped me in poverty to the very lips,
Given to captivity me and my utmost hopes,
I should have found in some place of my soul
A drop of patience. But, alas, to make me
The fixèd figure for the time of scorn
To point his slow and moving finger at!
Yet could I bear that too, well, very well.
But there where I have garnered up my heart,
Where either I must live or bear no life,
The fountain from the which my current runs
Or else dries up—to be discarded thence!
Or keep it as a cistern for foul toads
To knot and gender in! Turn thy complexion there,
Patience, thou young and rose-lipped cherubin,—
Ay, there, look grim as hell!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I hope my noble lord esteems me honest.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Oh, ay, as summer flies are in the shambles,
That quicken even with blowing. O thou weed,
Who art so lovely fair and smell’st so sweet
That the sense aches at thee, would thou hadst ne'er
been born!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:

been born!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Alas, what ignorant sin have I committed?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Was this fair paper, this most goodly book,
Made to write “whore” upon? What committed?
Committed? O thou public commoner!
I should make very forges of my cheeks
That would to cinders burn up modesty
Did I but speak thy deeds. What committed?
Heaven stops the nose at it and the moon winks,
The bawdy wind that kisses all it meets
Is hushed within the hollow mine of earth
And will not hear ’t. What committed!
Impudent strumpet!

That would to cinders burn up modesty
Did I but speak thy deeds. What committed?
Heaven stops the nose at it and the moon winks,
The bawdy wind that kisses all it meets
Is hushed within the hollow mine of earth
And will not hear ’t. What committed!
Impudent strumpet!

Made to write “whore” upon? What committed?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: By heaven, you do me wrong!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Are you not a strumpet?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
No, as I am a Christian.
If to preserve this vessel for my lord
From any other foul unlawful touch
Be not to be a strumpet, I am none.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
No, as I am a Christian.
If to preserve this vessel for my lord
From any other foul unlawful touch
Be not to be a strumpet, I am none.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What, not a whore?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: No, as I shall be saved.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Is ’t possible?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Oh, heaven forgive us!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I cry you mercy, then,
I took you for that cunning whore of Venice
That married with Othello.— You, mistress,
That have the office opposite to Saint Peter
And keep the gate of hell!

Enter EMILIA

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
You, you, ay, you!
We have done our course. There’s money for your pains.
I pray you, turn the key and keep our counsel.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Alas, what does this gentleman conceive?
How do you, madam? How do you, my good lady?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Alas, what does this gentleman conceive?
How do you, madam? How do you, my good lady?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Alas, what does this gentleman conceive?
How do you, madam? How do you, my good lady?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Faith, half asleep.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Good madam, what’s the matter with my lord?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: With who?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Why, with my lord, madam.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Who is thy lord?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: He that is yours, sweet lady.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
I have none. Do not talk to me, Emilia.
I cannot weep, nor answers have I none,
But what should go by water. Prithee, tonight
Lay on my bed my wedding sheets. Remember,
And call thy husband hither.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA

Exit

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
'Tis meet I should be used so, very meet.
How have I been behaved that he might stick
The small’st opinion on my least misuse?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
'Tis meet I should be used so, very meet.
How have I been behaved that he might stick
The small’st opinion on my least misuse?
Enter EMILIA with IAGO

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: What is your pleasure, madam? How is ’t with you?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: What is your pleasure, madam? How is ’t with you?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
I cannot tell. Those that do teach young babes
Do it with gentle means and easy tasks.
He might have chid me so, for, in good faith,
I am a child to chiding.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
I cannot tell. Those that do teach young babes
Do it with gentle means and easy tasks.
He might have chid me so, for, in good faith,
I am a child to chiding.

sentences.text:

I am a child to chiding.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: What is the matter, lady?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Alas, Iago, my lord hath so bewhored her,
Thrown such despite and heavy terms upon her,
That true hearts cannot bear it.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Am I that name, Iago?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: What name, fair lady?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Such as she says my lord did say I was.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
He called her “whore.” A beggar in his drink
Could not have laid such terms upon his callet.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Why did he so?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I do not know. I am sure I am none such.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I do not know. I am sure I am none such.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Do not weep, do not weep. Alas the day!

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Do not weep, do not weep. Alas the day!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Hath she forsook so many noble matches,
Her father and her country, and her friends,
To be called “whore”? Would it not make one weep?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Hath she forsook so many noble matches,
Her father and her country, and her friends,
To be called “whore”? Would it not make one weep?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: It is my wretched fortune.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Beshrew him for ’t!
How comes this trick upon him?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Beshrew him for ’t!
How comes this trick upon him?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Nay, heaven doth know.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
I will be hanged, if some eternal villain,
Some busy and insinuating rogue,
Some cogging, cozening slave, to get some office,
Have not devised this slander. I will be hanged else!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
I will be hanged, if some eternal villain,
Some busy and insinuating rogue,
Some cogging, cozening slave, to get some office,
Have not devised this slander. I will be hanged else!

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Fie, there is no such man. It is impossible.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Fie, there is no such man. It is impossible.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: If any such there be, heaven pardon him!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
A halter pardon him and hell gnaw his bones!
Why should he call her “whore?” Who keeps her company?
What place? What time? What form? What likelihood?
The Moor’s abused by some most villainous knave,
Some base notorious knave, some scurvy fellow.
O heavens, that such companions thou’dst unfold,
And put in every honest hand a whip
To lash the rascals naked through the world
Even from the east to th' west!

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Speak within door.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Oh, fie upon them! Some such squire he was
That turned your wit the seamy side without
And made you to suspect me with the Moor.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Oh, fie upon them! Some such squire he was
That turned your wit the seamy side without
And made you to suspect me with the Moor.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: You are a fool. Go to.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: You are a fool. Go to.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Alas Iago,
What shall I do to win my lord again?
Good friend, go to him. For, by this light of heaven,
I know not how I lost him. Here I kneel:
If e'er my will did trespass 'gainst his love,
Either in discourse of thought or actual deed,
Or that mine eyes, mine ears, or any sense,
Delighted them, or any other form,
Or that I do not yet, and ever did,
And ever will— though he do shake me off
To beggarly divorcement—love him dearly,
Comfort forswear me! Unkindness may do much,
And his unkindness may defeat my life,
But never taint my love. I cannot say “whore,”
It does abhor me now I speak the word.
To do the act that might the addition earn
Not the world’s mass of vanity could make me.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
I pray you, be content, ’tis but his humor.
The business of the state does him offence,
And he does chide with you.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:

The business of the state does him offence,
And he does chide with you.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: If ’twere no other—

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: 'Tis but so, I warrant.
Trumpets sound

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Hark, how these instruments summon to supper.
The messengers of Venice stays the meat.
Go in, and weep not. All things shall be well.

Exeunt DESDEMONA and EMILIA
Enter RODERIGO

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: How now, Roderigo!

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text: I do not find that thou deal’st justly with me.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: What in the contrary?

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
Every day thou daff’st me with some device, Iago, and
rather, as it seems to me now, keep’st from me all
conveniency than suppliest me with the least advantage
of hope. I will indeed no longer endure it, nor am I yet
persuaded to put up in peace what already I have
foolishly suffered.

persuaded to put up in peace what already I have

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Will you hear me, Roderigo?

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
I have heard too much, and your words and performances
are no kin together.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: You charge me most unjustly.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
With naught but truth. I have wasted myself out of my
means. The jewels you have had from me to deliver
Desdemona would half have corrupted a votaress. You have
told me she hath received them and returned me
expectations and comforts of sudden respect and
acquaintance, but I find none.

“Very well,” “go to”! I cannot go to, man, nor ’tis
not very well. Nay, I think it is scurvy, and begin to
find myself fopped in it.

find myself fopped in it.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
“Very well,” “go to”! I cannot go to, man, nor ’tis
not very well. Nay, I think it is scurvy, and begin to
find myself fopped in it.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Very well.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
I tell you ’tis not very well. I will make myself known
to Desdemona. If she will return me my jewels I will
give over my suit and repent my unlawful solicitation.
If not, assure yourself I will seek satisfaction of you.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
I tell you ’tis not very well. I will make myself known
to Desdemona. If she will return me my jewels I will
give over my suit and repent my unlawful solicitation.
If not, assure yourself I will seek satisfaction of you.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
I tell you ’tis not very well. I will make myself known
to Desdemona. If she will return me my jewels I will
give over my suit and repent my unlawful solicitation.
If not, assure yourself I will seek satisfaction of you.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
I tell you ’tis not very well. I will make myself known
to Desdemona. If she will return me my jewels I will
give over my suit and repent my unlawful solicitation.
If not, assure yourself I will seek satisfaction of you.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: You have said now.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
Ay, and said nothing but what I protest intendment of
doing.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Why, now I see there’s mettle in thee, and even from
this instant to build on thee a better opinion than ever
before. Give me thy hand, Roderigo. Thou hast taken
against me a most just exception, but yet I protest I
have dealt most directly in thy affair.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Why, now I see there’s mettle in thee, and even from
this instant to build on thee a better opinion than ever
before. Give me thy hand, Roderigo. Thou hast taken
against me a most just exception, but yet I protest I
have dealt most directly in thy affair.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Why, now I see there’s mettle in thee, and even from
this instant to build on thee a better opinion than ever
before. Give me thy hand, Roderigo. Thou hast taken
against me a most just exception, but yet I protest I
have dealt most directly in thy affair.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text: It hath not appeared.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
I grant indeed it hath not appeared, and your suspicion
is not without wit and judgment. But, Roderigo, if thou
hast that in thee indeed, which I have greater reason
to believe now than ever—I mean purpose, courage and
valor—this night show it. If thou the next night
following enjoy not Desdemona, take me from this world
with treachery and devise engines for my life.

sentences.text:

following enjoy not Desdemona, take me from this world

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text: Well, what is it? Is it within reason and compass?

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text: Well, what is it? Is it within reason and compass?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Sir, there is especial commission come from Venice to
depute Cassio in Othello’s place.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
Is that true? Why, then Othello and Desdemona return
again to Venice.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
Is that true? Why, then Othello and Desdemona return
again to Venice.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Oh, no, he goes into Mauritania and taketh away with
him the fair Desdemona, unless his abode be lingered
here by some accident—wherein none can be so determinate
as the removing of Cassio.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Oh, no, he goes into Mauritania and taketh away with
him the fair Desdemona, unless his abode be lingered
here by some accident—wherein none can be so determinate
as the removing of Cassio.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text: How do you mean, removing of him?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Why, by making him uncapable of Othello’s place:
knocking out his brains.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Why, by making him uncapable of Othello’s place:
knocking out his brains.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text: And that you would have me to do!

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Ay, if you dare do yourself a profit and a right. He
sups tonight with a harlotry, and thither will I go to
him. He knows not yet of his honorable fortune. If you
will watch his going thence (which I will fashion to
fall out between twelve and one) you may take him at
your pleasure. I will be near to second your attempt,
and he shall fall between us. Come, stand not amazed at
it, but go along with me. I will show you such a
necessity in his death that you shall think yourself
bound to put it on him. It is now high suppertime, and
the night grows to waste. About it!

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text: I will hear further reason for this.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: And you shall be satisfied.
Exeunt
Enter OTHELLO, LODOVICO, DESDEMONA, EMILIA and attendants

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: I do beseech you, sir, trouble yourself no further.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Oh, pardon me, ’twill do me good to walk.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Madam, good night. I humbly thank your ladyship.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Madam, good night. I humbly thank your ladyship.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Your honor is most welcome.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Will you walk, sir?—O Desdemona—

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Will you walk, sir?—O Desdemona—

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: My lord?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Get you to bed on th' instant, I will be returned
Forthwith. Dismiss your attendant there, look ’t be
done.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Get you to bed on th' instant, I will be returned
Forthwith. Dismiss your attendant there, look ’t be
done.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Get you to bed on th' instant, I will be returned
Forthwith. Dismiss your attendant there, look ’t be
done.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Get you to bed on th' instant, I will be returned
Forthwith. Dismiss your attendant there, look ’t be
done.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I will, my lord.
Exeunt OTHELLO, LODOVICO, and attendants

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: How goes it now? He looks gentler than he did.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: How goes it now? He looks gentler than he did.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
He says he will return incontinent,
And hath commanded me to go to bed
And bid me to dismiss you.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Dismiss me?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
It was his bidding. Therefore, good Emilia,
Give me my nightly wearing, and adieu.
We must not now displease him.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
It was his bidding. Therefore, good Emilia,
Give me my nightly wearing, and adieu.
We must not now displease him.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
It was his bidding. Therefore, good Emilia,
Give me my nightly wearing, and adieu.
We must not now displease him.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Ay. Would you had never seen him!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Ay. Would you had never seen him!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
So would not I. My love doth so approve him
That even his stubbornness, his checks, his frowns—
Prithee, unpin me—have grace and favor.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
So would not I. My love doth so approve him
That even his stubbornness, his checks, his frowns—
Prithee, unpin me—have grace and favor.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: I have laid those sheets you bade me on the bed.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
All’s one. Good Father, how foolish are our minds!
If I do die before thee, prithee, shroud me
In one of these same sheets.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
All’s one. Good Father, how foolish are our minds!
If I do die before thee, prithee, shroud me
In one of these same sheets.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
All’s one. Good Father, how foolish are our minds!
If I do die before thee, prithee, shroud me
In one of these same sheets.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Come, come! You talk!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Come, come! You talk!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
My mother had a maid called Barbary,
She was in love, and he she loved proved mad
And did forsake her. She had a song of “Willow,”
An old thing ’twas, but it expressed her fortune
And she died singing it. That song tonight
Will not go from my mind. I have much to do
But to go hang my head all at one side
And sing it like poor Barbary. Prithee, dispatch.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Shall I go fetch your nightgown?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
No, unpin me here.
This Lodovico is a proper man.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
No, unpin me here.
This Lodovico is a proper man.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: A very handsome man.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: He speaks well.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
I know a lady in Venice would have walked barefoot to
Palestine for a touch of his nether lip.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
( singing)
The poor soul sat sighing by a sycamore tree,
Sing all a green willow.
Her hand on her bosom, her head on her knee,
Sing willow, willow, willow.
The fresh streams ran by her, and murmured her moans,
Sing willow, willow, willow.
Her salt tears fell from her, and softened the stones
Sing willow, willow, willow—
Lay by these—
Willow, willow—
Prithee, hie thee, he’ll come anon—
Sing all a green willow must be my garland.
Let nobody blame him, his scorn I approve—
Nay, that’s not next—Hark! Who is ’t that knocks?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
(sings)
I called my love false love but what said he then?
Sing willow, willow, willow.
If I court more women you’ll couch with more men—
So, get thee gone, good night. Mine eyes do itch,
Doth that bode weeping?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: 'Tis neither here nor there.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
I have heard it said so. Oh, these men, these men!
Dost thou in conscience think—tell me, Emilia—
That there be women do abuse their husbands
In such gross kind?

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: EMILIA

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Wouldst thou do such a deed for all the world?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Why, would not you?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: No, by this heavenly light!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Nor I neither, by this heavenly light.
I might do ’t as well i' th' dark.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Nor I neither, by this heavenly light.
I might do ’t as well i' th' dark.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Wouldst thou do such a deed for all the world?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
The world’s a huge thing. It is a great price for a
small vice.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:

small vice.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: In troth, I think thou wouldst not.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
In troth, I think I should, and undo ’t when I had
done. Marry, I would not do such a thing for a
joint-ring, nor for measures of lawn, nor for gowns,
petticoats, nor caps, nor any petty exhibition. But for
the whole world? Why, who would not make her husband a
cuckold to make him a monarch? I should venture
purgatory for ’t.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Beshrew me, if I would do such a wrong
For the whole world.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Why the wrong is but a wrong i' th' world, and having
the world for your labor, ’tis a wrong in your own
world, and you might quickly make it right.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Why the wrong is but a wrong i' th' world, and having
the world for your labor, ’tis a wrong in your own
world, and you might quickly make it right.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I do not think there is any such woman.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Yes, a dozen, and as many to th' vantage as would store
the world they played for.
But I do think it is their husbands' faults
If wives do fall. Say that they slack their duties
And pour our treasures into foreign laps,
Or else break out in peevish jealousies,
Throwing restraint upon us. Or say they strike us,
Or scant our former having in despite.
Why, we have galls, and though we have some grace,
Yet have we some revenge. Let husbands know
Their wives have sense like them. They see and smell
And have their palates both for sweet and sour,
As husbands have. What is it that they do
When they change us for others? Is it sport?
I think it is. And doth affection breed it?
I think it doth. Is ’t frailty that thus errs?
It is so too. And have not we affections,
Desires for sport, and frailty, as men have?
Then let them use us well, else let them know,
The ills we do, their ills instruct us so.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Good night, good night. Heaven me such uses send,
Not to pick bad from bad, but by bad mend!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Good night, good night. Heaven me such uses send,
Not to pick bad from bad, but by bad mend!
Exeunt
Enter IAGO and RODERIGO

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Here, stand behind this bulk, straight will he come.
Wear thy good rapier bare, and put it home.
Quick, quick! Fear nothing. I’ll be at thy elbow.
It makes us, or it mars us. Think on that,
And fix most firm thy resolution.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text: Be near at hand, I may miscarry in ’t.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Here, at thy hand. Be bold, and take thy stand.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Here, at thy hand. Be bold, and take thy stand.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: Here, at thy hand. Be bold, and take thy stand.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:
I have no great devotion to the deed
And yet he hath given me satisfying reasons.
'Tis but a man gone. Forth, my sword: he dies.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO
sentences.text:

'Tis but a man gone. Forth, my sword: he dies.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
(aside) I have rubbed this young quat almost to the
sense,
And he grows angry. Now, whether he kill Cassio
Or Cassio him, or each do kill the other,
Every way makes my gain. Live Roderigo,
He calls me to a restitution large
Of gold and jewels that I bobbed from him
As gifts to Desdemona.
It must not be. If Cassio do remain
He hath a daily beauty in his life
That makes me ugly. And besides, the Moor
May unfold me to him—there stand I in much peril.
No, he must die. But so, I hear him coming.

sentences.speaker: RODERIGO

sentences.speaker: CASSIO

But that my coat is better than thou know’st
I will make proof of thine.

I will make proof of thine.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
'Tis he. Oh, brave Iago, honest and just,
That hast such noble sense of thy friend’s wrong!
Thou teachest me. Minion, your dear lies dead,
And your unblest fate hies. Strumpet, I come.
For, of my heart, those charms, thine eyes, are
blotted.
Thy bed, lust-stained, shall with lust’s blood be
spotted.

This is the night
That either makes me or fordoes me quite.

DESDEMONA asleep in bed Enter OTHELLO with a light
DESDEMONA asleep in bed Enter OTHELLO with a light

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
It is the cause, it is the cause, my soul.
Let me not name it to you, you chaste stars,
It is the cause. Yet I’ll not shed her blood,
Nor scar that whiter skin of hers than snow
And smooth as monumental alabaster.
Yet she must die, else she’ll betray more men.
Put out the light, and then put out the light.
If I quench thee, thou flaming minister,
I can again thy former light restore
Should I repent me. But once put out thy light,
Thou cunning’st pattern of excelling nature,
I know not where is that Promethean heat
That can thy light relume. When I have plucked thy rose
I cannot give it vital growth again,
It must needs wither. I’ll smell thee on the tree.
Oh, balmy breath, that dost almost persuade
Justice to break her sword! One more, one more.
Be thus when thou art dead and I will kill thee
And love thee after. (kissing her) One more, and that’s
the last.
So sweet was ne'er so fatal. I must weep,
But they are cruel tears. This sorrow’s heavenly,
It strikes where it doth love. She wakes.

And love thee after. (kissing her) One more, and that’s
the last.
So sweet was ne'er so fatal. I must weep,
But they are cruel tears. This sorrow’s heavenly,
It strikes where it doth love. She wakes.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
It is the cause, it is the cause, my soul.
Let me not name it to you, you chaste stars,
It is the cause. Yet I’ll not shed her blood,
Nor scar that whiter skin of hers than snow
And smooth as monumental alabaster.
Yet she must die, else she’ll betray more men.
Put out the light, and then put out the light.
If I quench thee, thou flaming minister,
I can again thy former light restore
Should I repent me. But once put out thy light,
Thou cunning’st pattern of excelling nature,
I know not where is that Promethean heat
That can thy light relume. When I have plucked thy rose
I cannot give it vital growth again,
It must needs wither. I’ll smell thee on the tree.
Oh, balmy breath, that dost almost persuade
Justice to break her sword! One more, one more.
Be thus when thou art dead and I will kill thee
And love thee after. (kissing her) One more, and that’s
the last.
So sweet was ne'er so fatal. I must weep,
But they are cruel tears. This sorrow’s heavenly,
It strikes where it doth love. She wakes.

sentences.text: Who’s there? Othello?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Who’s there? Othello?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Ay, Desdemona.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Will you come to bed, my lord?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Have you prayed tonight, Desdemon?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Ay, my lord.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
If you bethink yourself of any crime
Unreconciled as yet to heaven and grace,
Solicit for it straight.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Alack, my lord, what do you mean by that?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Well, do it, and be brief. I will walk by.
I would not kill thy unpreparèd spirit.
No, heavens forfend, I would not kill thy soul!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Talk you of killing?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Ay, I do.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Then heaven
Have mercy on me!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Amen, with all my heart.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: If you say, I hope you will not kill me.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Humh!

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
And yet I fear you, for you’re fatal then
When your eyes roll so. Why I should fear I know not,
Since guiltiness I know not. But yet I feel I fear.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Think on thy sins.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: They are loves I bear to you.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Ay, and for that thou diest.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
That death’s unnatural that kills for loving.
Alas, why gnaw you so your nether lip?
Some bloody passion shakes your very frame.
These are portents, but yet I hope, I hope
They do not point on me.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: I will so. What’s the matter?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
That handkerchief
Which I so loved and gave thee, thou gav’st
To Cassio.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
No, by my life and soul!
Send for the man and ask him.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
No, by my life and soul!
Send for the man and ask him.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Sweet soul, take heed, take heed of perjury.
Thou art on thy deathbed.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Sweet soul, take heed, take heed of perjury.
Thou art on thy deathbed.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Ay—but not yet to die!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Presently.
Therefore confess thee freely of thy sin,
For to deny each article with oath
Cannot remove nor choke the strong conception
That I do groan withal. Thou art to die.

And have you mercy too! I never did
Offend you in my life, never loved Cassio
But with such general warranty of heaven
As I might love. I never gave him token.

By heaven, I saw my handkerchief in ’s hand.
O perjured woman, thou dost stone my heart,
And mak’st me call what I intend to do
A murder, which I thought a sacrifice!
I saw the handkerchief.

He found it then,
I never gave it him. Send for him hither,
Let him confess a truth.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: What, my lord?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: That he hath usèd thee.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: How? Unlawfully?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: How? Unlawfully?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Ay.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: He will not say so.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
No, his mouth is stopped. Honest Iago
Hath ta'en order for ’t.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Oh! My fear interprets. What, is he dead?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Oh! My fear interprets. What, is he dead?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Oh! My fear interprets. What, is he dead?

Had all his hairs been lives
My great revenge had stomach for them all.

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text: Alas, he is betrayed and I undone.

sentences.text:
What noise is this?—Not dead? Not yet quite dead?
I that am cruel am yet merciful,
I would not have thee linger in thy pain.
So, so.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: [within] What, ho! My lord, my lord!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: [within] What, ho! My lord, my lord!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Who’s there?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: [within] O good my lord, I would speak a word with you!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Yes, ’tis Emilia.— (to EMILIA) By and by.— She’s dead.—
'Tis like she comes to speak of Cassio’s death,
The noise was high. Ha! No more moving?
Still as the grave. Shall she come in? Were ’t good?
I think she stirs again—No. What’s best to do?
If she come in, she’ll sure speak to my wife—
My wife! my wife! what wife? I have no wife.
Oh, insupportable! Oh, heavy hour!
Methinks it should be now a huge eclipse
Of sun and moon, and that th' affrighted globe
Should yawn at alteration.

[within]
I do beseech you
That I may speak with you, O good my lord!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I had forgot thee—Oh, come in, Emilia.
Soft, by and by. Let me the curtains draw.
Where art thou?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What’s the matter with thee now?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: O my good lord, yonder’s foul murders done!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What, now?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What, now?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: But now, my lord.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
It is the very error of the moon,
She comes more nearer earth than she was wont
And makes men mad.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Cassio, my lord, hath killed a young Venetian
Called Roderigo.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Roderigo killed? And Cassio killed!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Roderigo killed? And Cassio killed!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: No, Cassio is not killed.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO

Not Cassio killed! Then murder’s out of tune,
And sweet revenge grows harsh.

sentences.text: A guiltless death I die.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Oh, who hath done this deed?

sentences.speaker: DESDEMONA
sentences.text:
Nobody. I myself. Farewell.
Commend me to my kind lord. Oh, farewell!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Why, how should she be murdered?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Alas, who knows?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: You heard her say herself it was not I.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: She said so. I must needs report the truth.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: She said so. I must needs report the truth.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
She’s like a liar gone to burning hell.
'Twas I that killed her.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
She’s like a liar gone to burning hell.
'Twas I that killed her.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Oh, the more angel she,
And you the blacker devil!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: She turned to folly, and she was a whore.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Thou dost belie her, and thou art a devil.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: She was false as water.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Thou art rash as fire,
To say that she was false. Oh, she was heavenly true!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Thou art rash as fire,
To say that she was false. Oh, she was heavenly true!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Cassio did top her, ask thy husband else.
Oh, I were damned beneath all depth in hell,
But that I did proceed upon just grounds
To this extremity. Thy husband knew it all.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: That she was false to wedlock?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Ay, with Cassio. Had she been true,
If heaven would make me such another world
Of one entire and perfect chrysolite,
I’d not have sold her for it.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Ay, ’twas he that told me on her first.
An honest man he is, and hates the slime
That sticks on filthy deeds.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: What needs this iterance, woman? I say thy husband.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
O mistress, villainy hath made mocks with love!
My husband say that she was false!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO

He, woman.
I say “thy husband”—dost understand the word?
My friend, thy husband, honest, honest Iago.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
If he say so, may his pernicious soul
Rot half a grain a day! He lies to th' heart.
She was too fond of her most filthy bargain.

sentences.text: Ha!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Do thy worst.
This deed of thine is no more worthy heaven
Than thou wast worthy her.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Peace, you were best.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Thou hast not half that power to do me harm
As I have to be hurt. O gull! O dolt!
As ignorant as dirt! Thou hast done a deed—
I care not for thy sword, I’ll make thee known
Though I lost twenty lives.— Help! Help, ho! Help!
The Moor hath killed my mistress! Murder, murder!

As I have to be hurt. O gull! O dolt!
As ignorant as dirt! Thou hast done a deed—
I care not for thy sword, I’ll make thee known
Though I lost twenty lives.— Help! Help, ho! Help!
The Moor hath killed my mistress! Murder, murder!
Enter MONTANO, GRATIANO, and IAGO

sentences.speaker: MONTANO
sentences.text: What is the matter? How now, general?

sentences.speaker: MONTANO
sentences.text: What is the matter? How now, general?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Oh, are you come, Iago? You have done well,
That men must lay their murders on your neck.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Oh, are you come, Iago? You have done well,
That men must lay their murders on your neck.

sentences.speaker: GRATIANO
sentences.text: What is the matter?

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
(to IAGO) Disprove this villain, if thou be’st a man.
He says thou told’st him that his wife was false.
I know thou didst not, thou'rt not such a villain.
Speak, for my heart is full.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
I told him what I thought, and told no more
Than what he found himself was apt and true.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: But did you ever tell him she was false?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: I did.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
You told a lie, an odious, damnèd lie.
Upon my soul, a lie, a wicked lie.
She false with Cassio! Did you say with Cassio?

sentences.text:

sentences.text: Oh, heavens forfend!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: And your reports have set the murder on.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Nay, stare not, masters, it is true, indeed.

sentences.speaker: GRATIANO
sentences.text: 'Tis a strange truth.

sentences.speaker: MONTANO
sentences.text: Oh, monstrous act!

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
Villainy, villainy, villainy!
I think upon ’t, I think I smell ’t, Oh, villainy!
I thought so then, I’ll kill myself for grief.
Oh, villainy, villainy!

sentences.text: What, are you mad? I charge you, get you home.

Perchance, Iago, I will ne'er go home.

Nay, lay thee down and roar,
For thou hast killed the sweetest innocent
That e'er did lift up eye.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Oh, she was foul!—
I scarce did know you, uncle. There lies your niece,
Whose breath, indeed, these hands have newly stopped.
I know this act shows horrible and grim.

sentences.speaker: GRATIANO
sentences.text:
Poor Desdemon! I am glad thy father’s dead,
Thy match was mortal to him, and pure grief
Shore his old thread in twain. Did he live now,
This sight would make him do a desperate turn,
Yea, curse his better angel from his side
And fall to reprobation.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
'Tis pitiful, but yet Iago knows
That she with Cassio hath the act of shame
A thousand times committed. Cassio confessed it,
And she did gratify his amorous works
With that recognizance and pledge of love
Which I first gave her. I saw it in his hand,
It was a handkerchief, an antique token
My father gave my mother.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
' Twill out, ’twill out.—I peace?
No, I will speak as liberal as the north.
Let heaven and men and devils, let them all,
All, all cry shame against me, yet I’ll speak.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
O thou dull Moor! That handkerchief thou speak’st of
I found by fortune and did give my husband.
For often, with a solemn earnestness—
More than indeed belonged to such a trifle—
He begged of me to steal it.

O murderous coxcomb! What should such a fool
Do with so good a wife?

O murderous coxcomb! What should such a fool

sentences.text:
Are there no stones in heaven
But what serve for the thunder?—Precious villain!
He runs at IAGO. IAGO stabs EMILIA

sentences.speaker: GRATIANO
sentences.text: The woman falls! Sure he hath killed his wife.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text: Ay, ay. Oh, lay me by my mistress' side.

sentences.speaker: MONTANO
sentences.text:
'Tis a notorious villain. Take you this weapon,
Which I have recovered from the Moor.
Come, guard the door without. Let him not pass,
But kill him rather. I’ll after that same villain,
For ’tis a damnèd slave.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I am not valiant neither,
But ever puny whipster gets my sword.
But why should honor outlive honesty?
Let it go all.

sentences.speaker: EMILIA
sentences.text:
What did thy song bode, lady?
Hark, canst thou hear me? I will play the swan.
And die in music.
( singing) Willow, willow, willow —
Moor, she was chaste, she loved thee, cruel Moor.
So come my soul to bliss, as I speak true.
So speaking as I think, alas, I die.

sentences.text:
I have another weapon in this chamber,
It was a sword of Spain, the ice-brook’s temper.
Oh, here it is. Uncle, I must come forth.

sentences.speaker: GRATIANO
sentences.text:
[within] If thou attempt it, it will cost thee dear.
Thou hast no weapon, and perforce must suffer.

sentences.speaker: GRATIANO
sentences.text:
[within] If thou attempt it, it will cost thee dear.
Thou hast no weapon, and perforce must suffer.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Look in upon me then and speak with me,
Or, naked as I am, I will assault thee.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Look in upon me then and speak with me,
Or, naked as I am, I will assault thee.
Enter GRATIANO

sentences.speaker: GRATIANO
sentences.text: What is the matter?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Behold, I have a weapon.
A better never did itself sustain
Upon a soldier’s thigh. I have seen the day
That with this little arm and this good sword
I have made my way through more impediments
Than twenty times your stop. But, oh, vain boast!
Who can control his fate? ’tis not so now.
Be not afraid, though you do see me weaponed.
Here is my journey’s end, here is my butt,
And very sea-mark of my utmost sail.
Do you go back dismayed? ’tis a lost fear.
Man but a rush against Othello’s breast,
And he retires. Where should Othello go?—
Now, how dost thou look now? O ill-starred wench,
Pale as thy smock! When we shall meet at compt
This look of thine will hurl my soul from heaven
And fiends will snatch at it. Cold, cold, my girl,
Even like thy chastity. O cursed, cursed slave!
Whip me, ye devils,
From the possession of this heavenly sight!
Blow me about in winds, roast me in sulfur,
Wash me in steep-down gulfs of liquid fire!—
Oh, Desdemona! Desdemona! dead! Oh! Oh!

This look of thine will hurl my soul from heaven
And fiends will snatch at it. Cold, cold, my girl,
Even like thy chastity. O cursed, cursed slave!
Whip me, ye devils,
From the possession of this heavenly sight!
Blow me about in winds, roast me in sulfur,
Wash me in steep-down gulfs of liquid fire!—
Oh, Desdemona! Desdemona! dead! Oh! Oh!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Behold, I have a weapon.
A better never did itself sustain
Upon a soldier’s thigh. I have seen the day
That with this little arm and this good sword
I have made my way through more impediments
Than twenty times your stop. But, oh, vain boast!
Who can control his fate? ’tis not so now.
Be not afraid, though you do see me weaponed.
Here is my journey’s end, here is my butt,
And very sea-mark of my utmost sail.
Do you go back dismayed? ’tis a lost fear.
Man but a rush against Othello’s breast,
And he retires. Where should Othello go?—

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Where is this rash and most unfortunate man?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: That’s he that was Othello. Here I am.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: That’s he that was Othello. Here I am.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Where is that viper? Bring the villain forth.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Where is that viper? Bring the villain forth.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Where is that viper? Bring the villain forth.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I look down towards his feet, but that’s a fable.
If that thou be’st a devil, I cannot kill thee.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I look down towards his feet, but that’s a fable.
If that thou be’st a devil, I cannot kill thee.

sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Wrench his sword from him.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Wrench his sword from him.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text: I bleed, sir, but not killed.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I am not sorry neither. I’d have thee live,
For in my sense ’tis happiness to die.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I am not sorry neither. I’d have thee live,
For in my sense ’tis happiness to die.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
O thou Othello, thou was once so good,
Fall'n in the practice of a cursèd slave,
What shall be said to thee?

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
O thou Othello, thou was once so good,
Fall'n in the practice of a cursèd slave,
What shall be said to thee?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Why, anything.
An honorable murderer, if you will,
For naught I did in hate, but all in honor.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Why, anything.
An honorable murderer, if you will,
For naught I did in hate, but all in honor.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
This wretch hath part confessed his villainy.
Did you and he consent in Cassio’s death?

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
This wretch hath part confessed his villainy.
Did you and he consent in Cassio’s death?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Ay.

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text: Dear general, I never gave you cause.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I do believe it, and I ask your pardon.
Will you, I pray, demand that demi-devil
Why he hath thus ensnared my soul and body?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
I do believe it, and I ask your pardon.
Will you, I pray, demand that demi-devil
Why he hath thus ensnared my soul and body?

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Demand me nothing. What you know, you know.
From this time forth I never will speak word.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Demand me nothing. What you know, you know.
From this time forth I never will speak word.

sentences.speaker: IAGO
sentences.text:
Demand me nothing. What you know, you know.
From this time forth I never will speak word.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: What, not to pray?

sentences.speaker: GRATIANO
sentences.text: Torments will ope your lips.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Well, thou dost best.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
Sir, you shall understand what hath befall'n,
Which, as I think, you know not. Here is a letter
Found in the pocket of the slain Roderigo,
And here another. The one of them imports
The death of Cassio to be undertook
By Roderigo.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: Oh, villain!

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text: Most heathenish and most gross!

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
Now here’s another discontented paper
Found in his pocket too, and this, it seems,
Roderigo meant to have sent this damnèd villain
But that, belike, Iago in the interim
Came in and satisfied him.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
Now here’s another discontented paper
Found in his pocket too, and this, it seems,
Roderigo meant to have sent this damnèd villain
But that, belike, Iago in the interim
Came in and satisfied him.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
O thou pernicious caitiff!
How came you, Cassio, by that handkerchief
That was my wife’s?

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
O thou pernicious caitiff!
How came you, Cassio, by that handkerchief
That was my wife’s?

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text:
I found it in my chamber,
And he himself confessed but even now
That there he dropped it for a special purpose
Which wrought to his desire.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: O fool! fool! fool!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: O fool! fool! fool!

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text: O fool! fool! fool!

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text:
There is besides in Roderigo’s letter
How he upbraids Iago, that he made him
Brave me upon the watch, whereon it came
That I was cast. And even but now he spake,
After long seeming dead—Iago hurt him,
Iago set him on.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
(to OTHELLO) You must forsake this room and go with us.
Your power and your command is taken off
And Cassio rules in Cyprus. For this slave,
If there be any cunning cruelty
That can torment him much and hold him long,
It shall be his. You shall close prisoner rest
Till that the nature of your fault be known
To the Venetian state.—Come, bring him away.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
Soft you, a word or two before you go.
I have done the state some service, and they know ’t.
No more of that. I pray you, in your letters,
When you shall these unlucky deeds relate,
Speak of me as I am. Nothing extenuate,
Nor set down aught in malice. Then must you speak
Of one that loved not wisely, but too well.
Of one not easily jealous, but being wrought,
Perplexed in the extreme. Of one whose hand,
Like the base Indian, threw a pearl away
Richer than all his tribe. Of one whose subdued eyes,
Albeit unused to the melting mood,
Drop tears as fast as the Arabian trees
Their medicinal gum. Set you down this,
And say besides that in Aleppo once,
Where a malignant and a turbaned Turk
Beat a Venetian and traduced the state,
I took by the throat the circumcisèd dog,
And smote him, thus.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text: Oh, bloody period!

sentences.speaker: GRATIANO
sentences.text: All that is spoke is marred.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
[To DESDEMONA]
I kissed thee ere I killed thee. No way but this,
Killing myself, to die upon a kiss.

sentences.speaker: OTHELLO
sentences.text:
[To DESDEMONA]
I kissed thee ere I killed thee. No way but this,
Killing myself, to die upon a kiss.
Kisses DESDEMONA, dies

sentences.speaker: CASSIO
sentences.text:
This did I fear, but thought he had no weapon,
For he was great of heart.

sentences.speaker: LODOVICO
sentences.text:
(to IAGO)
O Spartan dog,
More fell than anguish, hunger, or the sea,
Look on the tragic loading of this bed.
This is thy work. The object poisons sight,
Let it be hid.— Gratiano, keep the house
And seize upon the fortunes of the Moor,
For they succeed on you.— T o you, lord governor,
Remains the censure of this hellish villain:
The time, the place, the torture. Oh, enforce it!
Myself will straight aboard, and to the state
This heavy act with heavy heart relate.
